Desperate to escape as dawn approaches, Muzan unleashes a massive shockwave that incapacitates Tanjiro and Obanai and disrupts the Ubuyashiki family's surveillance. As Muzan attempts to flee, Inosuke and Zenitsu intervene to stall him, enduring severe injuries to keep the Demon King from escaping. Tanjiro, suffering from a nervous system attack caused by Muzan, manages to stabilize himself by stabbing his own leg with his Nichirin sword to regain focus. Despite his exhaustion, Muzan is horrified to see the remaining Hashira—Sanemi, Giyu, and Gyomei—rising once more to join the fray, determined to hold him until the sun rises.
key_events: Muzan unleashes a shockwave attack that incapacitates Tanjiro and Obanai.
Inosuke and Zenitsu join the battle to prevent Muzan from escaping.
Tanjiro stabs himself with his Nichirin sword to counteract the effects of Muzan's nervous system attack.
Muzan reaches a state of physical exhaustion where he can no longer effectively use his techniques.
Sanemi, Giyu, and Gyomei rejoin the fight to stall Muzan until dawn.

summary_text: The battle against Muzan Kibutsuji intensifies as the Demon Slayers coordinate their final efforts. Inosuke and Zenitsu launch a combined assault, with Zenitsu utilizing 'Honoikazuchi no Kami' to strike Muzan, though the lack of a red blade limits the damage. As the trio struggles against Muzan's shockwaves and regenerative speed, Tanjiro employs a rapid succession of Sun Breathing forms to keep the Demon King at bay. Despite his exhaustion and physical collapse, Tanjiro manages to pin Muzan to a building using 'Sunflower Thrust'. As Muzan attempts to retaliate, he is intercepted by Mitsuri Kanroji, who rips off his arm, and Sanemi Shinazugawa, who severs his other arm and pins him. When Muzan's body splits open to unleash a lethal bite, Obanai Iguro intervenes to save Tanjiro. The chapter concludes with the Hashira holding Muzan in place as the sun begins to rise.
key_events: Zenitsu and Inosuke coordinate to land strikes on Muzan.
Tanjiro uses multiple Sun Breathing forms to pin Muzan to a building.
Mitsuri Kanroji and Sanemi Shinazugawa join the fray to disable Muzan's arms.
Obanai Iguro saves Tanjiro from a lethal bite by taking the blow himself.
The sun begins to rise, signaling the final phase of the battle.

summary_text: As dawn approaches, Muzan Kibutsuji attempts to escape the rising sun by unleashing a shockwave that destroys Tanjiro's left arm and blows back the Hashira. Tanjiro and Giyu combine their strength to turn their Nichirin blade bright red, inflicting severe burns on Muzan. To survive the sunlight, Muzan grotesquely transforms into a giant infant, enveloping Tanjiro within his flesh. Kiriya Ubuyashiki coordinates the remaining Demon Slayers and Kakushi to obstruct Muzan's path using vehicles and a tram. Despite the Hashira being at their physical limit, they continue to assault the demon to prevent him from burrowing into the ground. Ultimately, Tanjiro exerts his remaining strength to intensify the heat of his blade from within Muzan's body, leaving the demon helpless as the full sunlight causes him to begin disintegrating into ash.
key_events: Tanjiro loses his left arm to Muzan's shockwave.
Tanjiro and Giyu successfully turn their blade bright red to burn Muzan.
Muzan transforms into a giant infant to shield himself from the sun, absorbing Tanjiro.
The Kakushi and Hashira work together to physically block Muzan's path with a tram.
Tanjiro forces his blade to glow brighter from inside Muzan, accelerating the demon's disintegration as the sun rises.

summary_text: Following the sunrise, Muzan Kibutsuji’s remains disintegrate, marking the end of the millennium-long war against demons. As the surviving Demon Slayers and Kakushi process the victory, Kiriya Ubuyashiki collapses from exhaustion after ordering medical aid for the wounded. The chapter follows the final moments of several Hashira: Gyomei Himejima passes away peacefully after reuniting with the spirits of the children from his temple, who reveal they had tried to protect him; Mitsuri Kanroji and Obanai Iguro succumb to their injuries together, sharing a final confession of love and a promise to marry in their next life. Meanwhile, Sanemi Shinazugawa experiences a near-death encounter in the afterlife where his father forces him back to the land of the living. Elsewhere, Inosuke and Zenitsu are confirmed to be alive, though severely injured. The chapter concludes with a devastating discovery: Giyu Tomioka finds Tanjiro Kamado unresponsive, with no pulse or breath, just as Nezuko arrives on the battlefield.
key_events: Muzan Kibutsuji is completely destroyed by the sunrise.
Gyomei Himejima dies after reconciling with the spirits of his temple children.
Mitsuri Kanroji and Obanai Iguro die together while professing their love.
Sanemi Shinazugawa is forced back to life by his father during a near-death experience.
Inosuke and Zenitsu are confirmed to have survived the battle.
Giyu Tomioka discovers that Tanjiro Kamado has no signs of life.
Nezuko Kamado arrives at the scene of Tanjiro's body.

summary_text: As Muzan Kibutsuji disintegrates, he reflects on his life and the realization that while he is dying, the feelings of those he killed have conquered him. In a final act of malice, he transfers all of his blood into the dying Tanjiro Kamado, intending for him to become the ultimate Demon King capable of conquering the sun and destroying the Demon Slayer Corps. Tanjiro awakens as a demon, regenerating his lost arm and manifesting a mutated Demon Slayer mark. He immediately attacks the nearby Kakushi, forcing Giyu Tomioka to intervene. Although Tanjiro initially burns in the sunlight, he quickly develops an immunity to it, shocking the remaining slayers. As Giyu, Inosuke, and Zenitsu attempt to restrain him, Inosuke finds himself unable to deliver a killing blow to his friend, paralyzed by memories of their shared bond.
key_events: Muzan Kibutsuji transfers all his blood to Tanjiro Kamado before disintegrating.
Tanjiro transforms into a demon, regenerating his arm and gaining a mutated Demon Slayer mark.
Tanjiro demonstrates immunity to sunlight, becoming the 'King of Demons' Muzan envisioned.
Giyu Tomioka, Inosuke Hashibira, and Zenitsu Agatsuma attempt to stop Tanjiro.
Inosuke fails to strike Tanjiro down due to his emotional attachment to their friendship.

summary_text: Following his transformation into a demon, Tanjiro attacks his allies, prompting Nezuko to intervene by hugging him and pleading for him to return to his human self. Despite Tanjiro manifesting bone whips and a powerful energy blast—which Nezuko blocks at the cost of her own injury—Giyu, Inosuke, and Zenitsu realize that Tanjiro is subconsciously resisting his murderous urges to avoid killing his sister. Observing the struggle, Kanao Tsuyuri decides to use the final vial of human-restoration medicine created by Tamayo and Shinobu. Utilizing her 'Equinoctial Vermilion Eye' technique, Kanao successfully evades Tanjiro's counterattack and injects him with the cure, causing him to stir as the medicine begins to take effect.
key_events: Nezuko attempts to calm the demonified Tanjiro, who resists his urges despite causing her injury.
Tanjiro manifests Muzan-like bone whips and a destructive energy blast.
Giyu Tomioka realizes Tanjiro is fighting his demonic nature from within.
Kanao Tsuyuri uses the 'Flower Breathing: Final Form' to approach Tanjiro.
Kanao successfully injects Tanjiro with the human-restoration medicine while sustaining a slash injury.

summary_text: Trapped within his own subconscious, Tanjiro struggles against the influence of Muzan Kibutsuji, who attempts to manipulate him into remaining a demon by claiming his family is dead, his friends hate him, and that he should embrace immortality. Tanjiro rejects these lies, fueled by his memories and the support of his deceased comrades, who push him toward the light. As Nezuko and his surviving friends—Giyu, Zenitsu, and Inosuke—pull him back to reality, Tanjiro refuses Muzan's final desperate attempts to drag him into darkness. Tanjiro successfully regains his humanity and awakens, apologizing to his friends for his actions while a demon. The chapter concludes with the emotional realization that the battle is finally over, as the survivors celebrate Tanjiro's return and Yushiro mourns Tamayo.
key_events: Tanjiro rejects Muzan's psychological manipulation and attempts to remain a demon.
The spirits of deceased Demon Slayers and Tanjiro's friends help pull him out of his subconscious.
Tanjiro successfully regains his human form and consciousness.
Muzan Kibutsuji is definitively defeated.
The survivors reunite, and Yushiro confirms the end of the conflict.

summary_text: Three months after the defeat of Muzan Kibutsuji, the world is free of demons. Tanjiro Kamado recovers at the Butterfly Mansion, dealing with the permanent loss of function in his left arm and right eye. He reconciles with his past as a demon, comforted by Nezuko and memories of Yushiro’s admiration for his resilience. Kiriya Ubuyashiki officially disbands the Demon Slayer Corps, expressing deep gratitude to the surviving Hashira, Giyu Tomioka and Sanemi Shinazugawa. The survivors—including Tengen Uzui, the Rengoku family, and the swordsmiths—gather to celebrate their victory and pay respects to the fallen. Kanao Tsuyuri reveals she has retained some vision and is caring for Obanai’s snake, Kaburamaru. Sanemi shares a moment of kindness with Nezuko, reminding him of his late brother Genya. Finally, Tanjiro, Nezuko, Zenitsu, and Inosuke visit the graves of their comrades before returning to the Kamado family home to begin their lives as civilians.
key_events: Tanjiro confirms his permanent physical disabilities resulting from his time as a demon.
The Demon Slayer Corps is officially disbanded by Kiriya Ubuyashiki.
The survivors pay their respects to the fallen comrades at their graves.
Tanjiro, Nezuko, Zenitsu, and Inosuke move back to the Kamado family home to start a new life.

summary_text: Set in modern-day Japan, the final chapter reveals that the descendants and reincarnations of the Demon Slayer Corps members are living peaceful lives in a world free of demons. The narrative follows the daily routines of characters like Sumihiko Kamado, the great-grandson of Tanjiro and Kanao, as he parkours to school while being pursued by police officers Sanehiro and Genya Shinazugawa. Throughout the city, various familiar faces appear in new roles: Yoshiteru and Toko Agatsuma discuss their ancestors; Aoba Hashibira, a descendant of Inosuke and Aoi, gains fame for discovering the Blue Spider Lily; Yushiro continues to live as a demon, gaining worldwide acclaim for his paintings of Tamayo; and Kiriya Ubuyashiki is recognized as the oldest living person in Japan. The chapter concludes at the Kamado household, where a display of historical artifacts—including Tanjiro’s sword and a photograph of the survivors—serves as a final tribute to the sacrifices made to secure this peaceful future.
key_events: Sumihiko Kamado parkours through the city to reach school on time, evading police officers Sanehiro and Genya.
Yoshiteru and Toko Agatsuma discuss the history of the Demon Slayers and the concept of reincarnation.
Aoba Hashibira faces public criticism after the rare Blue Spider Lilies he discovered wither under his care.
Yushiro Yamamoto gains international fame as an artist for his hyper-realistic paintings of Tamayo.
The chapter showcases various reincarnations and descendants living ordinary lives, including the Ubuyashiki, Rengoku, and Kocho families.
The story ends with a glimpse of the Kamado family home, featuring a memorial display of Tanjiro's Nichirin blade and a photograph of the original Demon Slayer survivors.

summary_text: Megumi Fushiguro, a student at Tokyo Jujutsu High, is tasked with retrieving a special grade cursed object from Sugisawa Municipal High School. Meanwhile, Yuji Itadori, a physically gifted high schooler, spends his time with the Occult Research club and visits his dying grandfather, who urges him to use his strength to help others. When the seal on the cursed object—a finger of the legendary Ryomen Sukuna—is broken, it attracts dangerous cursed spirits to the school, endangering Yuji's friends. Yuji and Megumi rush to the school to intervene, but they are overwhelmed by the spirits. To save his friends and Megumi, Yuji consumes the cursed finger, gaining cursed energy but allowing Sukuna to incarnate within his body. Yuji manages to retain partial control, but Megumi, recognizing the threat posed by the resurrected Sukuna, prepares to exorcise Yuji.
key_events: Megumi Fushiguro arrives at Sugisawa High to locate a special grade cursed object.
Yuji Itadori's grandfather passes away after telling Yuji to help people.
The Occult Research club accidentally unseals Sukuna's finger, attracting cursed spirits.
Yuji and Megumi fight to rescue Yuji's friends from the invading curses.
Yuji consumes Sukuna's finger to gain the power necessary to fight the spirits.
Sukuna incarnates within Yuji, who manages to suppress the curse's control.
Megumi identifies Yuji as a threat and prepares to exorcise him.

summary_text: Yuji Itadori awakens in a sealed room under interrogation by Satoru Gojo, who informs him that his secret execution has been ordered by the Jujutsu High higher-ups. To prove he can suppress the curse Sukuna, Yuji allows Sukuna to take control for ten seconds, during which Sukuna briefly battles Gojo before Yuji successfully regains control. Impressed, Gojo convinces the higher-ups to delay the execution on the condition that Yuji consumes all of Sukuna's remaining fingers, effectively acting as a living vessel for the curse. After visiting his hospitalized friends and resolving to become a jujutsu sorcerer to protect others, Yuji consumes a second finger. He accepts his fate to eventually die after consuming all of Sukuna's parts, honoring his grandfather's final wish to die surrounded by people. The chapter concludes with Yuji and Megumi preparing to depart for Tokyo Jujutsu High.
key_events: Satoru Gojo interrogates Yuji and reveals his scheduled execution.
Yuji successfully demonstrates his ability to suppress Sukuna for ten seconds.
Gojo proposes a stay of execution if Yuji consumes all of Sukuna's fingers.
Yuji decides to become a jujutsu sorcerer to prevent others from suffering due to curses.
Yuji consumes a second finger and agrees to the mission of finding and eating the rest.

summary_text: Yuji Itadori arrives at Tokyo Jujutsu High, where he must undergo an interview with Principal Masamichi Yaga to determine his eligibility for enrollment. Yaga initially rejects Yuji, arguing that Yuji's motivation—fulfilling his grandfather's dying wish to help others—is insufficient for the dangerous life of a sorcerer. Yaga attacks Yuji with a Cursed Corpse to force him to confront his true resolve. Yuji ultimately declares that he is attending because he is the only one capable of containing Sukuna and he refuses to live a life of regret. Satisfied with this answer, Yaga accepts him into the school. Following the interview, Gojo reveals that Yuji's body acts as a radar for Sukuna's fingers, necessitating his involvement in future missions. The chapter concludes with Yuji settling into his dorm, preparing to meet the final first-year student, Nobara Kugisaki, the following day.
key_events: Yuji Itadori arrives at Tokyo Jujutsu High for his entrance interview.
Principal Masamichi Yaga tests Yuji's resolve by attacking him with a Cursed Corpse.
Yuji articulates his personal motivation to attend the school, moving past his grandfather's influence to claim his own agency.
Yuji is officially accepted into Jujutsu High.
Gojo reveals that Yuji's body can track the location of Sukuna's fingers.
Yuji and Megumi prepare to meet the third first-year student, Nobara Kugisaki.

summary_text: Yuji Itadori and Megumi Fushiguro meet up with Satoru Gojo at Harajuku Station, where they are introduced to the third first-year student, Nobara Kugisaki. To evaluate the abilities of his new students, Gojo takes Yuji and Nobara to an abandoned building near a cemetery infested with cursed spirits. Gojo instructs Yuji to refrain from using Sukuna's power and orders Megumi to stay behind, focusing the test on Nobara. Inside the building, Yuji and Nobara split up; Yuji successfully dispatches a curse using his physical prowess and the Slaughter Demon cursed tool. Meanwhile, Nobara encounters a cursed mannequin and realizes that the curses in Tokyo are significantly more powerful than those she faced in the countryside.
key_events: Introduction of Nobara Kugisaki as the third first-year student.
Gojo initiates a field test for Yuji and Nobara at an abandoned building.
Yuji successfully exorcises a curse using the Slaughter Demon knife.
Nobara faces a cursed mannequin and discovers the increased difficulty of urban curses.

summary_text: Nobara Kugisaki and Yuji Itadori clear an abandoned building of cursed spirits. During the mission, Nobara encounters a sentient curse holding a young boy hostage, forcing her to drop her guard. Yuji intervenes by smashing through a wall to rescue the boy, allowing Nobara to finish off the curse using her Straw Doll Technique. Following the battle, Nobara reveals to Yuji that her motivation for becoming a jujutsu sorcerer was to move to Tokyo and live authentically, inspired by a girl named Saori from her past. Meanwhile, a cursed womb begins to gestate a special grade cursed spirit, setting the stage for a future mission where one of three students will perish.
key_events: Nobara and Yuji exorcise cursed spirits in an abandoned house.
A sentient curse takes a young boy hostage to manipulate Nobara.
Yuji rescues the hostage, and Nobara successfully exorcises the curse using Resonance.
Nobara explains her personal motivations for becoming a sorcerer to Yuji.
A special grade cursed womb begins to manifest, foreshadowing a tragic future mission.

summary_text: Yuji, Megumi, and Nobara are deployed to the Eishu Juvenile Detention Center to rescue survivors from a cursed womb. After being briefed by Kiyotaka Ijichi and entering the facility, they discover they have been trapped within a curse's domain. The team finds the corpses of several inmates, including the son of the woman who requested their help. A moral conflict erupts between Yuji and Megumi regarding the value of saving lives, specifically those of criminals. The situation turns dire when Nobara is separated from the group and attacked by curses, while Megumi's Divine Dog is destroyed by a special grade cursed spirit. The chapter concludes with Yuji losing his hand and his weapon, the Slaughter Demon, during an initial confrontation with the special grade entity.
key_events: Deployment of Yuji, Megumi, and Nobara to the Eishu Juvenile Detention Center
Discovery of the cursed womb's domain
Discovery of the deceased inmates, including Tadashi Okazaki
Moral disagreement between Yuji and Megumi regarding the rescue mission
Separation of Nobara from the group
Destruction of Megumi's Divine Dog by a special grade curse
Yuji's initial engagement with the special grade curse resulting in the loss of his hand and weapon

summary_text: Trapped within a juvenile detention center by a special grade cursed spirit, Yuji and Megumi formulate a desperate plan to survive. Megumi uses his shikigami, Nue, to navigate the building and locate Nobara, who is being overwhelmed by lesser curses. Meanwhile, Yuji engages the special grade spirit alone to buy time, despite being severely outmatched and losing his hand. As Yuji struggles with his own perceived weakness, he attempts to channel cursed energy into a strike, though it proves ineffective. Upon hearing the howl of Megumi’s Divine Dog—the signal that Nobara has been rescued and they are ready to escape—Yuji relinquishes control to Sukuna, who emerges to confront the cursed spirit.
key_events: Megumi uses Nue to navigate the facility and locate Nobara.
Yuji fights the special grade cursed spirit alone, losing his hand in the process.
Megumi rescues Nobara from a group of cursed spirits using his Toad shikigami.
Yuji attempts to harness cursed energy to strike the special grade spirit.
Megumi signals Yuji by having his Divine Dog howl, prompting Yuji to unleash Sukuna.

summary_text: Yuji Itadori successfully switches consciousness with Sukuna to handle the special grade cursed spirit. Sukuna quickly dominates the curse, expressing disdain for being compared to such a weak entity. After healing Yuji's injuries, Sukuna unleashes his Domain Expansion, 'Malevolent Shrine,' to effortlessly slaughter the curse and retrieve one of his fingers. When Yuji fails to regain control of his body, Sukuna realizes he remains in command. He proceeds to rip out Yuji's heart, effectively holding Yuji's life hostage, as Sukuna can survive without it while Yuji cannot. After consuming a third finger, Sukuna confronts Megumi Fushiguro with the intent to kill him.
key_events: Sukuna takes control of Yuji's body to battle the special grade cursed spirit.
Sukuna uses Domain Expansion: Malevolent Shrine to kill the special grade curse.
Sukuna retrieves one of his fingers from the defeated curse's remains.
Sukuna rips out Yuji's heart to prevent him from regaining control and to hold his life hostage.
Sukuna consumes a third finger and prepares to attack Megumi Fushiguro.

summary_text: Megumi Fushiguro engages in a desperate battle against Sukuna, who has taken full control of Yuji Itadori's body. As Megumi struggles against Sukuna's overwhelming physical superiority, he reflects on his personal philosophy regarding fairness and his motivation for saving Yuji, which is rooted in his desire to help good people like his sister, Tsumiki. Megumi reaffirms his identity as a jujutsu sorcerer rather than a hero, stating he has no regrets about his decision to save Yuji. His resolve triggers a brief moment where Yuji regains control of his body; Yuji tells Megumi to live a long life before collapsing and dying.
key_events: Megumi Fushiguro fights Sukuna using his Ten Shadows Technique.
Megumi reflects on his past and his motivation for saving Yuji, citing his sister Tsumiki.
Megumi defines his role as a jujutsu sorcerer who saves people unfairly.
Yuji briefly regains control of his body from Sukuna.
Yuji tells Megumi to live a long life before dying.

summary_text: In the aftermath of the Cursed Womb incident, a group of cursed spirits led by Pseudo-Geto discuss their plan to eliminate humanity, revealing they used a Sukuna finger to test Yuji Itadori's vessel status. Satoru Gojo confronts the reality that the school's higher-ups intentionally sent his students on a suicide mission during his absence, while Shoko Ieiri prepares to perform an autopsy on Yuji's body. Meanwhile, Megumi and Nobara struggle with grief, leading them to accept an invitation from the second-year students—Maki, Toge, and Panda—to participate in the upcoming Kyoto Sister-School Goodwill Event as a means to grow stronger in Yuji's memory. The chapter concludes with Pseudo-Geto outlining a strategy to immobilize Gojo and recruit Sukuna, while Yuji is revealed to be alive within his own consciousness, confronting Sukuna.
key_events: Pseudo-Geto and a group of cursed spirits reveal they orchestrated the Cursed Womb incident to test Yuji.
Satoru Gojo deduces that the higher-ups intentionally sent his students to die.
The second-year students invite Megumi and Nobara to participate in the Kyoto Sister-School Goodwill Event.
Megumi and Nobara resolve to become stronger to honor Yuji.
Yuji is revealed to be alive within his inner consciousness, confronting Sukuna.

summary_text: Inside his Innate Domain, Yuji confronts Sukuna, who attempts to force a binding vow to revive him in exchange for control over Yuji's body whenever the word 'Enchain' is spoken. Yuji refuses, leading to a brief fight where Sukuna kills him again, though they eventually reach an agreement. In the real world, Satoru Gojo discusses his desire to reform the corrupt jujutsu society with Kiyotaka Ijichi, expressing his intent to foster strong allies like Yuta Okkotsu and Kinji Hakari. Yuji suddenly revives while Shoko Ieiri is preparing to dissect his body. Satoru decides to keep Yuji's survival a secret from the higher-ups to protect him while he undergoes training for the upcoming Kyoto Goodwill Event. Meanwhile, Suguru Geto informs Jogo that they cannot defeat Gojo through direct combat and proposes using the Prison Realm to trap him.
key_events: Yuji and Sukuna negotiate a binding vow within the Innate Domain.
Satoru Gojo reveals his ambition to reform the jujutsu world by training a new generation of powerful sorcerers.
Yuji is resurrected, and Satoru decides to hide his survival from the higher-ups.
Geto and Jogo discuss a plan to seal Satoru Gojo using the Prison Realm.

summary_text: The cursed spirit Jogo meets with Pseudo-Geto to discuss a plan to eliminate Satoru Gojo, with Jogo expressing confidence in his strength, which Geto estimates to be equivalent to eight or nine of Sukuna's fingers. During their meeting, Jogo incinerates the humans in a restaurant, causing a disturbance. Meanwhile, Megumi Fushiguro visits the mother of the late Tadashi Okazaki to return his nametag and apologize for failing to save him. At Jujutsu High, Maki Zenin, Panda, and Toge Inumaki oversee physical combat training for Megumi and Nobara Kugisaki. Simultaneously, Satoru Gojo begins training Yuji Itadori, explaining the difference between cursed energy and cursed techniques. Because Yuji lacks an innate technique, Gojo focuses on teaching him to control his cursed energy through emotional regulation, utilizing movies as a training tool.
key_events: Jogo and Pseudo-Geto conspire to kill Satoru Gojo.
Jogo massacres the humans in a restaurant to demonstrate his power.
Megumi visits Tadashi's mother to offer his condolences and return a personal item.
Maki, Panda, and Toge conduct close-quarters combat training for Megumi and Nobara.
Gojo begins training Yuji to harness and control cursed energy.

summary_text: Satoru Gojo initiates a unique training regimen for Yuji Itadori, requiring him to watch a variety of movies while maintaining a constant flow of cursed energy into a Cursed Corpse doll to keep it dormant. Simultaneously, Megumi Fushiguro trains with Maki Zenin and Panda, exploring the possibility of storing cursed tools within his shadow-based shikigami. After checking on Yuji—who remains unable to recall the terms of his pact with Sukuna—Gojo departs. While traveling, Gojo is ambushed by the unregistered special grade cursed spirit, Jogo. Gojo remains unfazed by Jogo's fire-based assault, recognizing the curse's high intelligence and power, and eagerly prepares to engage in battle.
key_events: Yuji begins emotional control training by watching movies while powering a Cursed Corpse.
Megumi explores the potential of storing weapons within his shadows.
Yuji fails to recall the details of his binding vow with Sukuna.
The special grade cursed spirit Jogo ambushes Satoru Gojo.
Gojo identifies Jogo as a highly intelligent, unregistered special grade curse and prepares to fight him.

summary_text: The special grade cursed spirit Jogo launches a surprise attack on Satoru Gojo, attempting to exploit the fact that Gojo has been lured out alone. Jogo utilizes his innate technique, Disaster Flames, to summon Ember Insects, but Gojo effortlessly neutralizes the assault. Gojo reveals the nature of his 'Infinity' technique, demonstrating that Jogo's attacks cannot physically reach him due to the space created by convergence and divergence. After pummeling Jogo, Gojo uses Cursed Technique Reversal: Red to blast the curse into a forest. Gojo then retrieves his student, Yuji Itadori, and brings him to the battlefield to observe the fight as a practical learning experience.
key_events: Jogo launches a surprise attack on Satoru Gojo using Ember Insects.
Gojo demonstrates the power of his Limitless technique, rendering Jogo's attacks ineffective.
Gojo uses Cursed Technique Reversal: Red to overpower Jogo.
Gojo brings Yuji Itadori to the scene to use the battle as a teaching moment.

summary_text: Satoru Gojo retrieves Yuji Itadori from his training to provide a live demonstration of a high-level jujutsu battle. They arrive at the battlefield where Jogo is waiting. Despite Jogo's threats and his attempt to intimidate Yuji, Gojo remains dismissive of the cursed spirit's strength. Jogo activates his Domain Expansion, 'Coffin of the Iron Mountain,' trapping them in a volcanic space where his attacks are guaranteed to hit. Gojo explains the mechanics of Domains to Yuji, noting that they grant improved stats and sure-hit effects that bypass his Infinity technique. To counter, Gojo removes his blindfold and activates his own Domain, 'Unlimited Void.' This overwhelms Jogo with infinite information, rendering him immobile. Gojo decapitates Jogo to begin an interrogation, while Kenjaku and Hanami observe the scene from a distance, debating whether to intervene.
key_events: Gojo brings Yuji to the battlefield to witness a Domain Expansion lesson.
Jogo activates his Domain Expansion: Coffin of the Iron Mountain.
Gojo explains the mechanics and advantages of Domain Expansions.
Gojo counters Jogo's domain by activating his own: Unlimited Void.
Gojo incapacitates Jogo and decapitates him for interrogation.
Kenjaku and Hanami observe the battle from afar.

summary_text: Following the confrontation with Satoru Gojo, Jogo is rescued by the cursed spirit Hanami, who uses a flower-based technique to create a distraction and escape with him. Satoru, impressed by the spirit's ability to mask its presence, resolves to accelerate Yuji Itadori's training to prepare him for future threats. Meanwhile, the mastermind Mahito convenes with his allies, including Pseudo-Geto, to finalize a plan to seal Satoru Gojo in Shibuya on October 31st. The chapter concludes at Tokyo Jujutsu High, where Kyoto students Aoi Todo and Mai Zenin arrive to intimidate Megumi and Nobara, mocking the late Yuji and demanding to see if the Tokyo students are worthy successors to Yuta Okkotsu.
key_events: Hanami rescues Jogo from Satoru Gojo using the 'Flower Field' technique.
The group of cursed spirits, led by Mahito and Pseudo-Geto, plot to seal Satoru Gojo in Shibuya on October 31st.
Satoru Gojo decides to intensify Yuji's training in preparation for the upcoming exchange event.
Kyoto Jujutsu High students Aoi Todo and Mai Zenin arrive at the Tokyo campus to confront and provoke Megumi and Nobara.

summary_text: Aoi Todo and Mai Zenin from Kyoto Jujutsu High arrive in Tokyo to scout their rivals. Aoi confronts Megumi Fushiguro, demanding to know his type in women; dissatisfied with Megumi's answer, Aoi labels him 'boring' and launches a brutal physical assault. Simultaneously, Mai engages Nobara Kugisaki, wounding her with a firearm. As Megumi attempts to defend himself using his Ten Shadows Technique, specifically the combined 'Well's Unknown Abyss,' he is overwhelmed by Aoi's superior speed and strength. The skirmish is abruptly halted by the arrival of second-year students Toge Inumaki and Panda, who force the Kyoto students to retreat. The chapter concludes with Maki Zenin intervening to protect Nobara from her sister, Mai.
key_events: Aoi Todo attacks Megumi Fushiguro after deeming his personality boring.
Mai Zenin engages Nobara Kugisaki and wounds her with a gun.
Megumi utilizes 'The Well's Unknown Abyss' to attempt to counter Aoi.
Toge Inumaki and Panda intervene to stop the fight between the schools.
Maki Zenin arrives to confront her sister, Mai, and protect Nobara.

summary_text: Following a confrontation between the Zenin sisters, Nobara Kugisaki recovers and pins Mai Zenin to the ground before Aoi Todo intervenes to escort Mai away. Maki Zenin reveals to Nobara that she possesses zero cursed energy and became a sorcerer to defy her critics, earning Nobara's respect. Meanwhile, Satoru Gojo confronts Principal Yoshinobu Gakuganji, challenging the higher-ups' decision to execute Yuji Itadori and warning that the next generation of cursed spirits and sorcerers will surpass current limitations. The chapter concludes one month later, as the cursed spirit Mahito leaves a crime scene involving murdered high school students, where he is spotted by a human and subsequently encountered by Yuji Itadori.
key_events: Nobara Kugisaki neutralizes Mai Zenin in a brief scuffle.
Aoi Todo arrives to collect Mai, warning of their intent to go all-out during the Kyoto Goodwill Event.
Maki Zenin explains her lack of cursed energy and her motivation for becoming a sorcerer to Nobara.
Satoru Gojo confronts Principal Gakuganji regarding the higher-ups' plot against Yuji and the evolution of intelligent cursed spirits.
Mahito commits a mass murder at a cinema and is spotted by a civilian.
Yuji Itadori arrives at the cinema to investigate the crime scene.

summary_text: At Kinema Cinema, Junpei Yoshino witnesses Mahito brutally transfigure and kill a group of rude classmates. Intrigued rather than horrified, Junpei follows Mahito into an alleyway and asks to learn how to kill people like him. Meanwhile, Grade 1 sorcerer Kento Nanami takes Yuji Itadori to the cinema to investigate the supernatural crime scene. Nanami instructs Yuji on tracking cursed energy residuals, maintaining a professional and detached demeanor. Satoru Gojo briefly appears to tease Nanami about his past as an office worker, highlighting Nanami's pragmatic view that sorcery is merely less idiotic than corporate life. Nanami refuses to acknowledge Yuji as a true sorcerer, citing his youth and the presence of Sukuna, and insists that protecting children is an adult's duty. The chapter concludes as Nanami and Yuji encounter two transfigured humans on the cinema roof, with Nanami preparing for battle.
key_events: Mahito kills Junpei's classmates using Idle Transfiguration.
Junpei Yoshino approaches Mahito to learn about cursed spirits and killing.
Kento Nanami mentors Yuji Itadori on tracking cursed energy residuals.
Satoru Gojo reveals Nanami's background as a former office worker.
Nanami and Yuji encounter transfigured humans at the cinema, initiating a battle.

summary_text: Kento Nanami and Yuji Itadori battle creatures at Kinema Cinema, where Nanami demonstrates his Ratio Technique by striking weak points on his targets. Yuji successfully utilizes his 'Divergent Fist'—a technique leveraging the time-lag of his cursed energy—to defeat his opponent. Nanami acknowledges Yuji's potential, but they soon discover the creatures are actually transmogrified humans. After consulting with Shoko Ieiri, they confirm these victims were killed by the shock of their physical alteration and were imbued with cursed energy. Disturbed by the discovery, Yuji and Nanami resolve to hunt down the perpetrator. Meanwhile, in an underground lair, the cursed spirit Mahito explains his origins to Junpei Yoshino, revealing that he was born from the hatred humans harbor toward one another.
key_events: Nanami demonstrates the Ratio Technique against the cinema creatures.
Yuji successfully executes the Divergent Fist technique.
Shoko Ieiri confirms the creatures are transmogrified humans, not cursed spirits.
Mahito reveals to Junpei that he was born from human hatred.

summary_text: In his underground lair, Mahito continues to manipulate Junpei Yoshino, showing him his grotesque human experiments and validating Junpei's nihilistic worldview regarding human indifference. Meanwhile, Yuji, Nanami, and Ijichi analyze the trail of disfiguration deaths in Kawasaki. Nanami deduces that the culprit is intentionally baiting them to their hideout; he decides to confront the threat alone to protect Yuji, tasking Yuji and Ijichi with investigating Junpei instead. As Yuji and Ijichi set out to test Junpei's jujutsu capabilities using low-level curses, Nanami infiltrates the lair, killing several of Mahito's transfigured humans and initiating a confrontation with the special grade curse.
key_events: Mahito shows Junpei his experiments on transfigured humans to reinforce Junpei's belief in human indifference.
Nanami, Yuji, and Ijichi triangulate the location of the culprit's hideout.
Nanami decides to confront the culprit alone, ordering Yuji to investigate Junpei's potential involvement instead.
Yuji and Ijichi prepare to test Junpei's ability to see curses.
Nanami infiltrates the underground lair and begins a battle against Mahito.

summary_text: Beneath Kawasaki City, Kento Nanami engages the special grade curse Mahito in combat. Nanami successfully lands a strike on Mahito, prompting the curse to reveal his Cursed Technique, Idle Transfiguration, which allows him to reshape the soul and, by extension, the physical body. Mahito demonstrates this by transfiguring a human into a monster, while Nanami remains focused on his strict work schedule, noting that he must finish the fight before his overtime begins. Meanwhile, above ground, Yuji Itadori intervenes in a tense confrontation between Junpei Yoshino and his teacher, Sotomura, after Ijichi accidentally releases cursed spirits too early. Yuji manages to distract the teacher and approaches Junpei to initiate contact. Back underground, Nanami’s cursed energy spikes as he enters his overtime period, signaling a shift in the intensity of his battle against Mahito.
key_events: Nanami battles Mahito and discovers his ability to reshape the soul.
Mahito reveals his Cursed Technique, Idle Transfiguration.
Yuji interrupts a confrontation between Junpei and his teacher, Sotomura.
Nanami enters his 'overtime' state, significantly increasing his cursed energy output.

summary_text: The battle between Kento Nanami and the curse Mahito reaches a turning point. Mahito injures Nanami by touching his soul, revealing that Nanami survives only by subconsciously guarding his soul with cursed energy. As the clock strikes overtime, Nanami removes his tie and unleashes his full power. Using his Ratio Technique: Collapse, he destroys the sewer environment to crush Mahito and severs one of the curse's legs before retreating. Meanwhile, Yuji Itadori attempts to befriend Junpei Yoshino, questioning him about the incident at the movie theater. Junpei lies to protect Mahito, but the two bond over their shared interest in cinema. Unbeknownst to them, Pseudo-Geto observes the interaction, satisfied that Mahito has successfully manipulated Junpei into befriending Sukuna's vessel.
key_events: Mahito injures Nanami's soul and explains the mechanics of his Idle Transfiguration.
Nanami enters 'overtime' mode, significantly increasing his cursed energy output.
Nanami uses Ratio Technique: Collapse to destroy the sewer structure and incapacitate Mahito.
Yuji Itadori questions Junpei Yoshino about the movie theater incident; Junpei chooses to lie.
Yuji and Junpei bond over their mutual interest in movies.
Pseudo-Geto observes Yuji and Junpei's interaction from afar.

summary_text: Yuji Itadori bonds with Junpei Yoshino over their shared interest in movies and joins him for dinner with his mother, Nagi. During their conversation, Yuji admits he is a jujutsu sorcerer and expresses his complex views on the necessity of killing. Meanwhile, Mahito survives his encounter with Kento Nanami, discovering he can maintain his soul's shape even when physically crushed. Nanami, recovering from his injuries, contacts Kiyotaka Ijichi for medical assistance and expresses grave concern that Mahito’s rapid growth could soon make him as dangerous as Jogo. The chapter concludes with a dark turn at the Yoshino household: after Yuji departs, Nagi discovers one of Sukuna’s fingers on her table, which inadvertently attracts a lethal cursed spirit to her location.
key_events: Yuji and Junpei bond over movies and share a dinner with Nagi Yoshino.
Mahito survives his battle with Nanami and realizes he can manipulate his soul's shape at will.
Nanami expresses fear that Mahito will become an unstoppable threat if he continues to grow in power.
Yuji reveals his status as a jujutsu sorcerer to Junpei.
Nagi Yoshino discovers one of Sukuna's fingers in her home, drawing a cursed spirit to her.

summary_text: Following the death of his mother, Nagi, due to a cursed spirit attracted by a Sukuna finger, Junpei Yoshino is manipulated by Mahito into seeking revenge. Mahito falsely convinces Junpei that a human enemy is responsible for the tragedy. While Yuji Itadori is instructed by Kento Nanami to continue monitoring Junpei rather than engaging in combat, Mahito initiates a plan to force Yuji into a binding vow. Mahito and Pseudo-Geto observe as Junpei arrives at his school and uses a Curtain to trap his classmates. Junpei confronts his bully, Shota Ito, and begins brutalizing him, believing him to be responsible for the curse. Yuji arrives at the scene, horrified by Junpei's actions, and attempts to intervene, but Junpei demands that he stay out of the conflict.
key_events: Mahito manipulates Junpei into believing a human is responsible for his mother's death.
Nanami instructs Yuji to monitor Junpei instead of fighting.
Mahito and Pseudo-Geto set a trap at Satozakura High School to force Yuji into a binding vow.
Junpei deploys a Curtain at his school and confronts Shota Ito.
Yuji arrives at the school and discovers Junpei in the middle of a violent assault.

summary_text: Ignoring direct orders from Nanami and Ijichi to remain on standby, Yuji Itadori confronts Junpei Yoshino at his school to prevent him from committing further acts of violence. During their battle, Yuji easily repels Junpei's shikigami, Moon Dregs, and uses his Divergent Fist to overwhelm him. Despite Junpei's nihilistic worldview and claims that people lack hearts, Yuji refuses to fight back, even allowing himself to be pierced by Junpei's shikigami to prove his sincerity. Yuji pleads with Junpei to abandon his path and join Jujutsu High so they can uncover the truth behind his mother's death together. Just as Junpei begins to waver, Mahito appears on the scene to intervene.
key_events: Yuji defies Nanami and Ijichi's orders to confront Junpei alone.
Yuji engages Junpei in combat, successfully countering his Moon Dregs shikigami.
Yuji attempts to reach Junpei emotionally, offering him a chance to join Jujutsu High to find his mother's killer.
Yuji intentionally takes a hit from Junpei's shikigami to demonstrate his lack of hostility.
Mahito arrives at the school to interrupt the confrontation between Yuji and Junpei.

summary_text: Mahito confronts Yuji and Junpei at Satozakura High School, revealing his true nature as a cursed spirit. Despite Junpei's misplaced trust, Mahito betrays him, using Idle Transfiguration to mutate his body into a monster and forcing him to attack Yuji. Yuji pleads with Sukuna to heal Junpei, but the King of Curses mocks him, revealing that he has no intention of helping. After Junpei dies from the aggressive transfiguration, Yuji is consumed by a dark, unprecedented rage. He lands a powerful punch on Mahito that causes the spirit to bleed, leading Mahito to realize that Yuji has gained the ability to perceive the contours of the soul due to his shared existence with Sukuna. The chapter concludes with Yuji declaring his intent to kill Mahito, fully embracing his role as a sorcerer.
key_events: Mahito reveals his identity and pins Yuji to a wall.
Mahito uses Idle Transfiguration to mutate Junpei into a monster.
Sukuna refuses to heal Junpei and mocks Yuji's desperation.
Junpei dies from his injuries.
Yuji lands a soul-damaging blow on Mahito, revealing his newfound ability to perceive the soul.
Yuji vows to kill Mahito.

summary_text: Driven by rage over Junpei's death, Yuji Itadori engages Mahito in a fierce battle at Satozakura High School. Mahito attempts to force Yuji to rely on Sukuna by pushing him to his limits, but the plan backfires when Mahito accidentally touches Sukuna's soul during an attempted Idle Transfiguration. Sukuna harshly warns Mahito never to touch his soul again, leaving Mahito momentarily vulnerable. Yuji capitalizes on this opening with a series of physical strikes, refusing to switch with Sukuna and vowing to kill Mahito himself. As the fight intensifies and Mahito prepares a lethal counterattack with a shapeshifted mace, Kento Nanami arrives on the scene and intervenes, deflecting Mahito's strike to save Yuji.
key_events: Yuji engages Mahito in combat, utilizing his physical strength to counter Mahito's soul-manipulation abilities.
Mahito attempts to use Idle Transfiguration on Yuji, but is stopped and threatened by Sukuna for touching his soul.
Yuji rejects the idea of relying on Sukuna, declaring his intent to kill Mahito personally.
Kento Nanami arrives at the battlefield and saves Yuji from a lethal attack by Mahito.

summary_text: Nanami joins Yuji in the battle against Mahito at Satozakura High School. After confirming Yuji's status and the safety of the unconscious students, Nanami realizes that Mahito is unable to use his Idle Transfiguration on Yuji, providing them a tactical advantage. The duo coordinates their attacks to overwhelm Mahito, forcing the cursed spirit to use transfigured humans as shields to distract Yuji. Yuji eventually overcomes his hesitation and kills the transfigured humans, allowing him to reunite with Nanami and land a decisive blow on Mahito. Pushed to the brink of death, Mahito experiences a breakthrough in his soul manipulation, allowing him to successfully activate his Domain Expansion, 'Self-Embodiment of Perfection,' and trap Nanami inside.
key_events: Nanami joins Yuji to fight Mahito.
Nanami and Yuji coordinate a tag-team assault to pressure Mahito.
Yuji kills the transfigured humans to clear his path.
Yuji and Nanami land a combined strike on Mahito.
Mahito achieves a breakthrough in his soul manipulation while near death.
Mahito activates his Domain Expansion: Self-Embodiment of Perfection.

summary_text: Trapped within Mahito's Domain Expansion, Kento Nanami reflects on his past as a disillusioned salaryman who left Jujutsu High because he found the work meaningless. He recalls a pivotal moment at a bakery where he exorcised a minor curse from a shop girl; her genuine gratitude gave him a sense of purpose, leading him to return to the jujutsu world. In the present, Nanami prepares to die, but Yuji Itadori forcibly breaks into the Domain. Mahito's attempt to touch Yuji's soul triggers Ryomen Sukuna, who retaliates by slashing Mahito, shattering the Domain, and leaving the cursed spirit severely wounded.
key_events: Nanami reflects on his transition from a corporate banker back to a jujutsu sorcerer after experiencing true gratitude.
Yuji Itadori breaks into Mahito's Domain Expansion.
Sukuna attacks Mahito for touching his soul, destroying the Domain and incapacitating Mahito.

summary_text: Following his failed attempt to kill Mahito, Yuji Itadori collapses from his injuries while the cursed spirit escapes into the sewers. Mahito, shaken by his encounter with Sukuna's soul, vows to eventually torture Yuji further. After recovering in a remote house, Yuji struggles with the guilt of killing humans and his uncertainty regarding the meaning of a 'natural death.' Nanami comforts him, acknowledging Yuji as a true jujutsu sorcerer and encouraging him to continue his path despite the burden. Meanwhile, at Junpei's school, the teacher Sotomura and the bully Shota Ito confront their shared culpability in the events that led to Junpei's downfall. The chapter concludes with Yuji reuniting with Gojo and Nanami, resolving to grow stronger and defeat Mahito.
key_events: Mahito uses a decoy body to escape from Yuji and Nanami.
Nanami attempts to coordinate an interception of Mahito via Takuma Ino, but the pursuit is halted by Yuji's collapse.
Mahito reflects on the power of Sukuna's soul and plans future revenge against Yuji.
Nanami officially acknowledges Yuji as a jujutsu sorcerer and offers him guidance on the nature of death.
Sotomura and Shota Ito acknowledge their roles in the psychological destruction of Junpei Yoshino.
Yuji resolves to defeat Mahito and continues his training.

summary_text: Following his battle with Yuji and Nanami, Mahito regroups with Jogo and Geto, confirming their intent to revive Sukuna by manipulating Jujutsu High into gathering the cursed fingers for them. Meanwhile, at Tokyo Jujutsu High, the students prepare for the Kyoto Sister School Goodwill Event. Gojo and Yuji devise a plan to surprise the other students with Yuji's return, but the reveal falls flat as the Kyoto students are preoccupied with souvenirs and Yuji's classmates are more shocked and annoyed than celebratory. Gojo uses the opportunity to taunt Principal Gakuganji regarding Yuji's survival, while the Kyoto students assess the Tokyo team's capabilities, specifically noting Megumi's status as a Zenin.
key_events: Mahito, Jogo, and Geto finalize their plan to use Jujutsu High to collect Sukuna's fingers.
The Tokyo and Kyoto students gather for the Sister School Goodwill Event.
Gojo attempts to surprise the students with Yuji's return, but the reveal is poorly received.
The Kyoto students arrive and begin sizing up the Tokyo team, with Noritoshi Kamo expressing interest in Megumi's lineage.

summary_text: As the Kyoto Sister-School Goodwill Event approaches, the Tokyo and Kyoto teams prepare for the upcoming team battle. The Tokyo students regroup, with Yuji hiding the fact that he broke Maki's cursed tool, Slaughter Demon. Megumi notes that Yuji would win in a physical brawl without cursed energy. Meanwhile, the Kyoto team, under Principal Gakuganji's orders, plots to assassinate Yuji, the vessel of Sukuna, during the event. Aoi Todo dismisses the Kyoto team's schemes and leaves, while the others discuss strategies to counter Toge Inumaki's Cursed Speech. Elsewhere, Satoru Gojo tasks Utahime Iori with investigating the Kyoto campus for a potential mole within Jujutsu High, insulting her strength in the process. The chapter concludes with the Tokyo students bonding over their shared resolve to win, despite Yuji still processing the trauma of Junpei Yoshino's death.
key_events: Principal Yaga explains the rules of the Goodwill Event's team battle.
The Tokyo team assesses Yuji's physical capabilities and prepares for the competition.
Principal Gakuganji orders the Kyoto students to kill Yuji during the event.
Aoi Todo refuses to participate in the Kyoto team's assassination plot.
Satoru Gojo and Utahime Iori discuss the possibility of a traitor within the school.
Yuji and Megumi share a moment of mutual resolve regarding their past losses.

summary_text: The Kyoto Sister-School Goodwill Event commences after Satoru Gojo pranks Utahime Iori during her opening announcement. As the Tokyo team begins their strategy to track curses and split into squads, Aoi Todo immediately charges their position. Yuji Itadori steps forward to intercept Todo, fulfilling the Tokyo team's pre-planned strategy to stall the powerhouse. Despite Todo's overwhelming combat prowess and physical strength, Yuji manages to survive the initial onslaught. After a brief exchange of blows, Todo pauses the fight to ask Yuji a peculiar question: what type of girl he likes.
key_events: Satoru Gojo pranks Utahime Iori during the event's opening announcement.
The Tokyo team begins the Spirit Bash Race and executes their plan to split into squads.
Aoi Todo launches a direct assault on the Tokyo team.
Yuji Itadori engages Aoi Todo in a one-on-one fight to allow his teammates to escape.
Aoi Todo stops the fight to ask Yuji about his romantic preferences.

summary_text: During the Kyoto Sister School Goodwill Event, Aoi Todo confronts Yuji Itadori and asks about his romantic preferences. When Yuji reveals he likes tall girls with big butts, Aoi experiences a false, vivid memory of a shared middle school friendship, leading him to declare Yuji his 'best friend.' Consequently, Aoi turns against his own Kyoto teammates, who are actively attempting to assassinate Yuji under orders. Aoi uses his cursed technique, Boogie Woogie, to swap positions and force his classmates to retreat so he can fight Yuji one-on-one. Meanwhile, the Tokyo students intervene; Megumi uses Nue to ground Momo Nishimiya, while Maki and Megumi ambush the retreating Kyoto team, leading to direct confrontations between Maki and Kasumi, and Megumi and Noritoshi Kamo.
key_events: Aoi Todo forms a delusional 'best friend' bond with Yuji Itadori after discovering they share the same taste in women.
Aoi Todo turns against the Kyoto team to protect Yuji from their assassination attempt.
Aoi Todo uses his cursed technique, Boogie Woogie, to disrupt the Kyoto team's formation.
Megumi Fushiguro and Maki Zenin ambush the Kyoto students to defend Yuji.
Megumi confronts Noritoshi Kamo regarding the Kyoto team's intent to murder Yuji.

summary_text: The Tokyo team deduces that the Kyoto students are attempting to assassinate Yuji under orders from Principal Gakuganji, who views Sukuna's vessel as a threat. While Panda, Nobara, and Toge coordinate to neutralize the grade 2 cursed spirit and regroup, Megumi and Maki head back to protect Yuji. Meanwhile, Yuji continues his intense duel with Aoi Todo. Aoi, impressed by Yuji's raw power and tactical awareness, criticizes Yuji's reliance on 'Divergent Fist,' warning him that he will remain weak if he continues to rely on it. Elsewhere, Maki confronts Kasumi Miwa, who reveals her motivation for participating is to earn a higher salary for her family, though Maki remains determined to win. The chapter concludes with Yuji accepting Aoi's challenge to improve his technique, signaling a shift in their dynamic.
key_events: The Tokyo team realizes the Kyoto team is under orders to kill Yuji.
Panda and Nobara confront Momo Nishimiya after she is grounded by Nue.
Maki Zenin faces off against Kasumi Miwa.
Aoi Todo critiques Yuji's Divergent Fist and pushes him to evolve his combat style.
Yuji accepts Aoi's challenge to overcome his current limitations.

summary_text: During their duel, Aoi Todo mentors Yuji Itadori, explaining that Yuji's 'Divergent Fist' is ineffective against high-level opponents because his cursed energy lags behind his physical speed. Aoi teaches Yuji to stop viewing his body as separate parts and instead unify his mind, body, and soul to achieve a seamless flow of cursed energy. Yuji begins to grasp this concept, pushing him toward a new level of power. Elsewhere, Nobara Kugisaki confronts Momo Nishimiya, threatening the Kyoto students for their attempt on Yuji's life. Simultaneously, Panda reveals he was feigning injury to ambush Mechamaru, leading to a direct confrontation between the two cursed corpses.
key_events: Aoi Todo critiques Yuji's Divergent Fist and explains the necessity of synchronized cursed energy flow.
Yuji begins to understand how to unify his cursed energy with his physical movements.
Nobara confronts Momo regarding the Kyoto school's order to assassinate Yuji.
Panda reveals he faked an injury to surprise attack Mechamaru.
A battle begins between Panda and Mechamaru.

summary_text: The battle between Panda and the remote-controlled Ultimate Mechamaru intensifies as Mechamaru reveals his semi-grade 1 status and significant firepower. Kokichi Muta, the sorcerer controlling Mechamaru from a remote location, harbors deep resentment toward Panda, viewing him as a mere doll. Kokichi reveals his Heavenly Restriction, which grants him immense cursed energy at the cost of a frail, incomplete body, fueling his jealousy toward Panda's sentient existence. During the fight, Panda realizes Mechamaru is being controlled from afar and decides to end the match. After taking a direct hit from Mechamaru's 'Ultimate Cannon' to protect Nobara, Panda emerges from the smoke, revealing his true nature and his combat-focused 'Gorilla Mode'.
key_events: Mechamaru reveals his semi-grade 1 rank and utilizes various combat modes like Ultra Spin and Ultra Cannon.
Kokichi Muta's backstory is revealed, detailing his Heavenly Restriction and physical disabilities.
Panda explains his origin as an Abrupt-Mutation cursed corpse created by Principal Yaga.
Panda takes a full-power blast from the Ultimate Cannon to shield Nobara.
Panda reveals his 'Gorilla Mode' to counter Mechamaru.

summary_text: Panda reveals the nature of his existence, explaining that he is a Cursed Corpse with three internal cores—his own and those of his 'older brother' and 'sister.' During his battle with Mechamaru, Panda utilizes his Gorilla Mode to bypass Mechamaru's Ultra Shield with his Unblockable Drumming Beat. Despite Mechamaru's attempt to strike Panda's core, Panda reveals he can shift his core's location by concentrating his cursed energy, allowing him to land a decisive blow that destroys Mechamaru's puppet body. Following the fight, the two share a moment of mutual understanding regarding their status as outsiders; Panda expresses a desire to help Kokichi, who reveals his deep-seated wish to one day walk among his peers in a healthy body. Elsewhere, Maki Zenin continues her assault on Kasumi Miwa, who is overwhelmed by Maki's immense physical strength.
key_events: Panda reveals he possesses three energy cores, including those of his 'siblings'.
Panda defeats Mechamaru using Gorilla Mode and the Unblockable Drumming Beat.
Panda demonstrates the ability to shift his core's location to deceive opponents.
Panda and Kokichi Muta share a moment of empathy regarding their non-human status.
Maki Zenin overwhelms Kasumi Miwa with her superior combat skills.

summary_text: During the Kyoto Goodwill Event, Kasumi Miwa realizes that Maki Zenin’s combat prowess far exceeds the dismissive descriptions provided by Mai Zenin. Despite Kasumi activating her 'New Shadow Style: Simple Domain' to defend herself, Maki skillfully disarms her and gains the upper hand. Meanwhile, in the viewing room, Mei Mei and Satoru Gojo observe the fight and discuss the Zenin family's refusal to promote Maki, with Mei Mei noting her indifference toward non-monetary motivations. Elsewhere, Nobara Kugisaki pursues Momo Nishimiya, who uses her broom to maintain aerial superiority and launch wind-based attacks. Momo attempts to lecture Nobara on the systemic hardships and discrimination faced by female sorcerers like Mai, but Nobara dismisses the lecture as annoying.
key_events: Maki Zenin defeats Kasumi Miwa in combat by disarming her and neutralizing her Simple Domain.
Mei Mei and Satoru Gojo discuss the politics of the Zenin family and Maki's rank.
Momo Nishimiya engages Nobara Kugisaki in a battle while lecturing her on the difficulties of being a female sorcerer.
Panda successfully exorcises a curse, as noted by the observers in the viewing room.

summary_text: During the Kyoto Sister School Goodwill Event, Nobara Kugisaki engages in a heated debate with Momo Nishimiya regarding the Zenin family's sexist treatment of Maki and Mai. Nobara rejects Momo's assertion that their difficult upbringing justifies their behavior, arguing that personal character matters more than misfortune. Nobara successfully traps Momo using her Straw Doll Technique: Resonance on a bristle from Momo's broom, gaining the upper hand. However, just as Nobara is about to secure victory, Mai Zenin intervenes by shooting Nobara in the temple with a rubber bullet. Following the skirmish, Maki Zenin arrives to confront her sister, Mai, demanding that she address her as 'big sis' as they prepare to face off.
key_events: Nobara uses Straw Doll Technique: Hairpin to destroy the environment and close the distance on Momo.
Nobara uses Straw Doll Technique: Resonance on a broom bristle to incapacitate Momo.
Mai Zenin shoots Nobara in the temple with a rubber bullet, ending the fight between Nobara and Momo.
Maki Zenin arrives to confront Mai, initiating a one-on-one sisterly duel.

summary_text: During the Kyoto Sister School Goodwill Event, Maki Zenin faces her sister, Mai, in a decisive battle. As they fight, the narrative explores their strained childhood in the Zenin family, revealing Mai's deep-seated resentment toward Maki for leaving her behind to pursue a life as a sorcerer. Maki effortlessly dominates the physical confrontation, showcasing her superior combat skills. Mai attempts to surprise her sister by revealing her cursed technique, 'Construction,' which allows her to create a bullet at the cost of immense physical strain, but Maki catches the projectile with ease. The battle concludes with Maki's victory and a final emotional confrontation where Mai accuses Maki of being a liar for abandoning her, revealing that Mai never wanted to be a sorcerer and only pursued that path because of her sister's departure.
key_events: Maki Zenin and Mai Zenin engage in a combat encounter during the Goodwill Event.
Mai Zenin reveals her cursed technique, 'Construction,' to surprise Maki.
Maki Zenin demonstrates her superior physical prowess and reflexes by catching Mai's constructed bullet.
The sisters' shared history and the reasons behind Maki leaving the Zenin family are revealed through flashbacks.
Maki defeats Mai, leading to an emotional final exchange regarding their divergent paths and mutual resentment.

summary_text: The Kyoto Sister School Goodwill Event continues as Megumi Fushiguro and Noritoshi Kamo engage in a fierce duel. Noritoshi utilizes his Blood Manipulation technique, specifically the Flowing Red Scale ability, to enhance his physical prowess and control blood-tipped arrows, forcing Megumi onto the defensive. Meanwhile, Toge Inumaki uses Cursed Speech to incapacitate Kasumi Miwa and encounters a powerful cursed spirit, which is suddenly destroyed by the arrival of the special grade curse, Hanami. Behind the scenes, Principal Gakuganji has orchestrated a plot to have Yuji Itadori killed by a Semi-Grade 1 curse, while Mahito and a new curse user, Juzo Kumiya, prepare to execute their own malicious agenda against the jujutsu students.
key_events: Megumi Fushiguro and Noritoshi Kamo clash using their respective inherited techniques.
Noritoshi reveals the capabilities of Flowing Red Scale, allowing him to manipulate his own physiology.
Toge Inumaki uses Cursed Speech to put Kasumi Miwa to sleep.
Principal Gakuganji reveals his plan to have Yuji Itadori assassinated by a curse.
Hanami appears on the battlefield, and the curse user Juzo Kumiya is introduced as part of an enemy plot.

summary_text: Noritoshi Kamo and Megumi Fushiguro engage in a fierce duel, with Noritoshi revealing his motivation to become the Kamo family heir to protect his mother. He attempts to relate to Megumi, viewing them both as future pillars of the sorcerer clans, but Megumi rejects this ideology, asserting that he acts only on his own conscience and is committed to saving Yuji Itadori. Megumi utilizes his newly tamed shikigami, Max Elephant, to blast Noritoshi out of the temple, followed by an attack from Nue. As the two prepare to continue their fight, they are suddenly interrupted by a massive surge of wooden branches. Toge Inumaki arrives and uses Cursed Speech to command them to run away. Simultaneously, the curse user Juzo Kumiya erects a curtain over the battlefield, signaling the start of an external invasion.
key_events: Noritoshi Kamo reveals his backstory and motivation for becoming the Kamo family heir.
Megumi Fushiguro rejects the values of the sorcerer clans and reaffirms his resolve to save Yuji.
Megumi summons Max Elephant to attack Noritoshi.
Toge Inumaki interrupts the fight and commands Megumi and Noritoshi to flee.
Juzo Kumiya erects a curtain, marking the beginning of an enemy infiltration.

summary_text: The Kyoto Goodwill Event is thrown into chaos when a mysterious curtain is erected over the Tokyo Jujutsu High campus, specifically designed to exclude Satoru Gojo while allowing others to pass. Inside the viewing room, staff realize an intruder has infiltrated the school, prompting Gojo, Gakuganji, and Utahime to attempt a rescue mission for the students, while Mei Mei remains behind to monitor the situation via her crows. Meanwhile, Megumi, Toge, and Noritoshi Kamo are confronted by the special grade cursed spirit Hanami. Hanami, who claims to be the personification of the planet's wrath, declares his intent to eliminate humans to allow the Earth to recover. Despite a coordinated assault by the three students, Hanami proves resilient, forcing the group into a desperate battle.
key_events: A specialized curtain is cast over Tokyo Jujutsu High to trap the students and exclude Satoru Gojo.
Jujutsu High staff discover an intruder has infiltrated the school grounds.
Megumi, Toge, and Noritoshi Kamo engage the special grade cursed spirit Hanami.
Hanami reveals his ideology regarding the protection of the planet from humanity.
The students attempt a combined attack using Cursed Speech, Blood Manipulation, and the Ten Shadows Technique, but fail to significantly damage Hanami.

summary_text: As the Kyoto Goodwill Event is infiltrated, Utahime and Principal Gakuganji encounter the curse user Juzo Kumiya, leading Gakuganji to prepare for battle. Meanwhile, Megumi, Noritoshi, and Toge struggle to survive against the special grade cursed spirit Hanami. Despite Toge using Cursed Speech to create openings for Noritoshi’s Piercing Blood, Toge’s throat reaches its limit, allowing Hanami to overwhelm the group and defeat Nue. Toge makes a final stand, using a powerful command to blast Hanami back before collapsing. Maki Zenin arrives to join the fight, breaking her sword against Hanami's hide, but Megumi manages to strike Hanami's eye-socket weakness. The chapter concludes with Megumi providing Maki with the powerful cursed tool, Playful Cloud, to continue the battle.
key_events: Principal Gakuganji prepares to fight the curse user Juzo Kumiya.
Toge Inumaki reaches his physical limit using Cursed Speech against Hanami.
Hanami defeats Nue and pummels Noritoshi Kamo.
Toge successfully lands a final, powerful Cursed Speech command on Hanami.
Maki Zenin joins the fight and breaks her sword against Hanami.
Megumi identifies Hanami's eye socket as a weak point and equips Maki with the cursed tool Playful Cloud.

summary_text: Maki Zenin and Megumi Fushiguro team up to battle the special grade cursed spirit Hanami. Maki utilizes the special grade cursed tool 'Playful Cloud' to land powerful blows, while Megumi summons 'Divine Dog: Totality,' a powerful fusion form created after the death of his white Divine Dog. Despite their coordinated efforts and tactical weapon swapping, Hanami proves resilient, wounding Maki and infecting Megumi with a 'Cursed Sprout' that forces his shikigami to dispel. As Hanami prepares to finish off a restrained Maki, Yuji Itadori and Aoi Todo arrive on the scene, forcing Hanami back and taking over the fight.
key_events: Maki Zenin engages Hanami using the special grade cursed tool Playful Cloud.
Megumi Fushiguro summons Divine Dog: Totality to overwhelm Hanami with speed and power.
Maki and Megumi execute a coordinated weapon-swap attack, wounding Hanami.
Megumi is infected by a Cursed Sprout, causing his shikigami to vanish.
Hanami captures Maki, but Yuji Itadori and Aoi Todo arrive to rescue her and confront the cursed spirit.

summary_text: Momo Nishimiya evacuates the unconscious Toge Inumaki and Noritoshi Kamo from the battlefield, placing her trust in Aoi Todo's strength. Aoi and Yuji Itadori arrive to relieve the injured Maki Zenin and Megumi Fushiguro, with Panda assisting in the evacuation of the wounded. Aoi declares that he will not intervene in the fight against the special grade curse Hanami unless Yuji successfully performs 'Black Flash,' a technique involving the application of cursed energy within 0.000001 seconds of a physical strike. Yuji initially struggles to focus due to his anger regarding the curses' actions, leading to a reprimand from Aoi. After learning to suppress his emotions and concentrate, Yuji enters a state of intense focus and successfully lands a Black Flash on Hanami, surprising the curse with the sudden surge in power.
key_events: Momo Nishimiya evacuates Toge Inumaki and Noritoshi Kamo.
Aoi Todo and Yuji Itadori take over the fight against Hanami, allowing Panda to evacuate Maki and Megumi.
Aoi sets a condition for Yuji to master Black Flash or face death.
Yuji attempts to fight Hanami but fails to land a clean hit due to emotional distraction.
Aoi coaches Yuji on suppressing his anger to achieve the necessary focus.
Yuji successfully lands a Black Flash on Hanami.

summary_text: Following his successful use of Black Flash, Yuji Itadori experiences a significant surge in power, finally grasping the true essence of cursed energy. Aoi Todo mentors Yuji, comparing his growth to a chef mastering his ingredients. As they press their advantage against the special grade cursed spirit Hanami, the spirit is forced to heal its injuries and remove its robe, revealing its true combat form. Hanami unleashes a barrage of wooden attacks and Cursed Buds, but the duo manages to evade and counter-attack with coordinated strikes. As the battle intensifies, Hanami recalls Mahito’s advice to embrace his instincts, leading the spirit to find genuine enjoyment in the thrill of combat. The chapter concludes with Hanami using a field of flowers to distract the sorcerers, prompting Todo to prepare to unleash his own cursed technique.
key_events: Yuji Itadori achieves a new level of strength after landing a Black Flash.
Hanami heals from his injuries and removes his robe to fight more seriously.
Yuji and Todo coordinate their attacks to pressure Hanami.
Hanami begins to enjoy the thrill of battle after recalling advice from Mahito.
Todo decides to activate his cursed technique to turn the tide of the fight.

summary_text: The chapter begins with a flashback to Aoi Todo's childhood, where he encounters Yuki Tsukumo, who asks him about his preferences in women, sparking his belief that his life would eventually become interesting. In the present, Aoi and Yuji Itadori coordinate their assault against the cursed spirit Hanami. Aoi reveals his cursed technique, 'Boogie Woogie,' which allows him to swap positions with anyone by clapping his hands. By utilizing this technique to constantly switch places with Yuji, they disorient Hanami and land a series of devastating blows. Yuji enters 'the zone'—a state where using Black Flash becomes instinctive—and successfully lands four consecutive Black Flashes on Hanami, aided by a clever feint from Aoi who tricks the spirit into anticipating a swap that never occurs.
key_events: Flashback to Aoi Todo's childhood meeting with Yuki Tsukumo.
Aoi Todo reveals his cursed technique, 'Boogie Woogie,' which swaps the positions of two targets upon a clap.
Aoi and Yuji coordinate their movements to overwhelm Hanami.
Yuji Itadori enters 'the zone' and lands four consecutive Black Flashes on Hanami.
Aoi uses a psychological feint by clapping without activating his technique to ensure Yuji's final Black Flash connects.

summary_text: The battle between Yuji Itadori, Aoi Todo, and the special grade curse Hanami reaches its climax. Hanami attempts to overwhelm the duo by launching Cursed Buds, but Aoi successfully defends against them by momentarily disengaging his cursed energy protection after a mental visualization of his idol, Takada-chan. Aoi reveals the full versatility of his technique, Boogie Woogie, which allows him to swap positions with anything possessing cursed energy. Utilizing this, Aoi swaps Yuji with the special grade cursed tool Playful Cloud, which had been left in the river. Armed with the tool, Aoi lands a devastating blow on Hanami's face, exploiting a weakness previously identified by Megumi Fushiguro. As Hanami prepares to activate his Domain Expansion to secure a victory, the curtain surrounding the school is suddenly dispelled, and Satoru Gojo arrives on the battlefield, effectively ending the confrontation.
key_events: Aoi Todo successfully defends against Hanami's Cursed Buds.
Aoi uses Boogie Woogie to retrieve the cursed tool Playful Cloud.
Aoi lands a powerful strike on Hanami's face using Playful Cloud.
Hanami attempts to activate his Domain Expansion.
Satoru Gojo arrives on the battlefield, causing the curtain to fall.

summary_text: The Kyoto Goodwill Event battle reaches a turning point as Satoru Gojo arrives on the battlefield. While Utahime Iori is ambushed by a new curse user named Haruta Shigemo, she is saved by the arrival of Nobara Kugisaki and Mai Zenin, causing the attacker to flee. Gojo quickly intervenes in the fight between Yoshinobu Gakuganji and the curse user Juzo Kumiya, brutally immobilizing Juzo by crushing his limbs. Recognizing that the special grade curse Hanami is attempting to retreat, Gojo unleashes his Hollow Technique: Purple, a massive blast that obliterates a significant portion of the forest. Meanwhile, Mahito successfully completes his mission by infiltrating a school vault, murdering two staff members, and stealing cursed objects before escaping.
key_events: Utahime Iori is attacked by Haruta Shigemo but rescued by Nobara and Mai.
Satoru Gojo arrives and instantly incapacitates Juzo Kumiya.
Gojo uses Hollow Technique: Purple to destroy a large section of the forest in an attempt to eliminate Hanami.
Mahito successfully steals cursed objects from the Tokyo Jujutsu High vault and kills two staff members.

summary_text: Following the Kyoto Goodwill Event, the antagonist group successfully retreats with their spoils. Mahito retrieves a heavily injured Hanami and confirms the theft of six of Sukuna's fingers and the Death Painting Wombs (1-3) from the Jujutsu High vault. It is revealed that the group bypassed Tengen's barrier by tracking cursed energy on a finger and using a distraction to evade Satoru Gojo. The villains confirm their ultimate objective: sealing Gojo in Shibuya on October 31st. At Jujutsu High, the administration considers canceling the remainder of the Goodwill Event due to the security breach, but Gojo insists on continuing. The event is ultimately reorganized into a baseball game between the Tokyo and Kyoto students. Meanwhile, Megumi recovers from his injuries and expresses a renewed determination to surpass Yuji, while Yuji continues to be pursued by an overly affectionate Aoi Todo.
key_events: Mahito and the curse users successfully steal six Sukuna fingers and the Death Painting Wombs.
The antagonists reveal their plan to seal Satoru Gojo in Shibuya on October 31st.
Jujutsu High officials confirm the security breach and the involvement of Mahito.
The Kyoto Goodwill Event is officially converted into a baseball game.
Megumi Fushiguro resolves to surpass Yuji Itadori in strength.

summary_text: Following the chaotic events of the Kyoto Goodwill Event, Satoru Gojo and the students convene to decide the fate of the competition. Despite the recent violence, Aoi Todo successfully argues for the event's continuation, leading Gojo to replace the remaining combat portions with a game of baseball. During the match, Yuji and Noritoshi Kamo share a moment of mutual understanding regarding their motivations for becoming sorcerers. As the game progresses, the Tokyo students utilize teamwork to gain an advantage, while Maki Zenin intentionally hits an unpopular Todo with a pitch. Meanwhile, principals Gakuganji and Yaga discuss the moral implications of Yuji's existence, with Yaga emphasizing the importance of supporting the students despite the risks. The chapter concludes with Yuji hitting a home run, securing a victory for Tokyo High and officially ending the Goodwill Event.
key_events: Aoi Todo convinces the students to continue the Goodwill Event.
The event is reorganized into a baseball game.
Yuji Itadori and Noritoshi Kamo discuss their motivations for being sorcerers.
Principals Gakuganji and Yaga debate the ethics of Yuji's role as Sukuna's vessel.
Tokyo High wins the baseball game, concluding the Goodwill Event.

summary_text: Following the Goodwill Event, Yuji, Megumi, and Nobara are assigned a mission by assistant manager Akari Nitta to investigate a series of mysterious deaths. The victims, who all attended the same junior high school, were killed at their front doors under similar circumstances. The investigation leads the trio to Megumi’s former school, where they learn about a local paranormal hotspot, Yasohachi Bridge, known for student bungee jumping incidents. During the visit, Megumi reveals to his peers that he has a sister named Tsumiki. Meanwhile, Mahito and Pseudo-Geto discuss the nature of the Death Painting Wombs. Mahito forces one of the wombs into a human host, successfully manifesting it as a cursed spirit, just as the Jujutsu High students arrive at the bridge to begin their investigation.
key_events: Jujutsu High first-years are assigned to investigate a string of mysterious deaths linked to Megumi's former junior high.
The team visits Megumi's old school and identifies Yasohachi Bridge as a potential site of cursed activity.
Megumi reveals the existence of his sister, Tsumiki, to Yuji and Nobara.
Mahito and Pseudo-Geto discuss the properties of the Death Painting Wombs.
Mahito forces a Death Painting Womb into a human host, causing it to manifest as a cursed spirit.

summary_text: Mahito manifests a Death Painting Womb, tasking it with errands. Meanwhile, Megumi, Yuji, and Nobara investigate the Yasohachi Bridge curse. Megumi learns from a local that his sister, Tsumiki, visited the bridge, putting her at risk of the curse's lethal influence. Fearing for his friends' safety, Megumi attempts to handle the mission alone, but Yuji and Nobara confront him, insisting on helping and reaffirming their bond. The trio deduces the conditions to enter the curse's Domain—crossing under the bridge at night—and successfully infiltrate it. Upon entry, they are ambushed by both the bridge curse and the newly manifested Death Painting Womb, Kechizu. The group splits up to engage the threats, with Yuji taking on the Death Painting while Megumi and Nobara face the bridge curse.
key_events: Mahito successfully incarnates a Death Painting Womb.
Megumi discovers his sister Tsumiki is a potential victim of the Yasohachi Bridge curse.
Megumi attempts to exclude Yuji and Nobara from the mission to protect them.
Yuji and Nobara convince Megumi to let them assist, strengthening their team dynamic.
The trio successfully enters the curse's Domain by fulfilling specific ritualistic conditions.
The group is confronted by both the bridge curse and the Death Painting Womb, Kechizu.

summary_text: The first-year students split up to confront the curses at Yasohachi Bridge. Yuji engages the Death Painting Womb, Kechizu, who utilizes acidic blood attacks. Meanwhile, Megumi and Nobara battle a tunneling curse; Nobara uses her Straw Doll Technique to target the curse's exit points, while Megumi realizes the curse's barrier and range are draining its main body. The situation escalates when a new entity, Eso, pulls Nobara out of the barrier, prompting Yuji to pursue them. Megumi successfully exorcises the tunneling curse using Divine Dog: Totality, but discovers the Domain is still active. He realizes the case is linked to Sukuna's incarnation, as a new Special Grade Finger Bearer appears carrying the Sukuna finger that the Death Painting Wombs are seeking.
key_events: Yuji battles the Death Painting Womb, Kechizu.
