Mikasa attempts to execute both traitors, severely wounding them, but they successfully transform into their Titan forms. The Armored Titan grabs Eren while the Colossus Titan snatches Ymir. Driven by betrayal, Eren transforms into his Titan form to engage the Armored Titan in combat.
key_events: Hannes confirms there is no breach in Wall Rose, causing the Survey Corps to call off their operation.
Reiner Braun confesses his and Bertolt Hoover's identities as the Armored and Colossus Titans to Eren.
Armin recalls evidence suggesting Reiner and Bertolt aided the Female Titan during the expedition.
Mikasa attacks Reiner and Bertolt, wounding them before they transform.
Reiner and Bertolt transform into their Titan forms and attempt to kidnap Eren and Ymir.
Eren transforms into his Titan form to fight the Armored Titan.

summary_text: The battle between the Survey Corps and the traitors begins in earnest. Eren transforms into the Attack Titan and engages the Armored Titan, though his initial strikes prove ineffective against Reiner's hardened armor. Meanwhile, the Colossus Titan, Bertolt, captures Ymir and another soldier, consuming them. When the Survey Corps attempts to strike the Colossus Titan's nape, Bertolt releases a massive burst of high-pressure steam, forcing the soldiers to retreat and wait for the steam to dissipate. As Eren recovers from Reiner's brutal counterattacks, he reflects on his past training with Reiner, realizing that Reiner had been holding back and feeling deep disgust for the betrayal that led to the fall of Wall Maria. The chapter concludes with the Armored Titan continuing to overpower Eren, inflicting severe damage to his head.
key_events: Eren engages the Armored Titan in combat.
The Colossus Titan captures Ymir and a soldier, consuming them.
The Survey Corps fails to kill the Colossus Titan due to his defensive steam release.
Hange orders a standby strategy to wait for the Colossus Titan's steam to subside.
Eren realizes Reiner had been holding back during their training days.
The Armored Titan severely injures Eren, damaging his head.

summary_text: During his brutal fight against the Armored Titan, Eren recalls training sessions where Annie Leonhart taught him specialized hand-to-hand combat techniques. Applying these moves in the present, Eren successfully counters Reiner's brute strength with tactical strikes, throws, and submissions, eventually ripping off the Armored Titan's arm. Hange Zoë identifies that the Armored Titan's joints cannot be hardened, allowing Mikasa to slice through Reiner's legs while Eren holds him in a guillotine choke. As Eren prepares to rip the nape from the weakened Armored Titan, Reiner lets out a desperate roar for help. The chapter concludes with Bertolt Hoover, in his Colossus Titan form, falling from the top of the Wall to crush Eren and Reiner.
key_events: Eren recalls Annie Leonhart teaching him martial arts during their training days.
Eren uses Annie's techniques to gain the upper hand against the Armored Titan.
Hange Zoë discovers that the Armored Titan's joints are a structural weakness because they cannot be hardened.
Mikasa successfully damages the Armored Titan's legs.
Reiner calls for help, prompting the Colossus Titan to fall from the Wall onto the combatants.

summary_text: Following the battle between the Attack Titan and the Armored Titan, the Colossus Titan falls from the Wall, creating a massive explosion and steam cloud that allows Reiner and Bertolt to kidnap Eren and Ymir. In Trost, Commanders Pixis and Erwin coordinate with the Military Police to address the crisis, confirming that three members of the 104th Training Corps are Titans. Meanwhile, at Wall Rose, a recovering Mikasa and Armin lament the five-hour delay in launching a rescue mission due to the injuries of their superiors and the lack of horses. Hannes consoles them, vowing to help them track down Eren. The Survey Corps eventually mobilizes, with Hange deducing that the traitors have taken their captives to a nearby Titan forest. The chapter concludes with Eren and Ymir regaining consciousness in the forest under the watch of Reiner and Bertolt.
key_events: The Colossus Titan falls from the Wall, creating a diversion to facilitate the kidnapping of Eren and Ymir.
Commanders Erwin and Pixis successfully mobilize the Military Police to assist in the Titan crisis.
The Survey Corps confirms that three members of the 104th Training Corps are traitors.
Hannes joins Mikasa and Armin in their resolve to rescue Eren.
Hange identifies the location of the traitors as a nearby Titan forest.
Eren and Ymir wake up in the forest as prisoners of Reiner and Bertolt.

summary_text: Eren and Ymir awaken in a Titan Forest, held captive by Reiner and Bertolt. Eren, having lost his arms in the previous battle, is unable to transform as his body is focused on regeneration. Ymir warns him that escape is impossible due to the surrounding abnormal Titans and the fact that only their captors possess vertical maneuvering equipment. As they wait for nightfall to depart for the traitors' hometown, Reiner begins to suffer a mental breakdown, oscillating between his identity as a soldier and his true role as a 'Warrior.' Eren confronts them as mass murderers, specifically recalling the day his mother was killed. Ymir probes Reiner and Bertolt about the Beast Titan, noting their previous excitement toward the creature. Reiner attempts to recruit Ymir to their side by promising that joining them is the only way to ensure the safety of Historia Reiss, leaving Eren frustrated and without answers regarding the true nature of their enemy.
key_events: Eren and Ymir are held captive by Reiner and Bertolt in a forest filled with abnormal Titans.
Reiner experiences a psychological split, confusing his identity as a soldier with his mission as a Warrior.
Eren confronts Reiner and Bertolt, labeling them as mass murderers for their role in the destruction of Wall Maria.
Ymir questions the pair about the Beast Titan, revealing their interest in the creature.
Reiner attempts to manipulate Ymir into joining them by offering to protect Historia Reiss.

summary_text: In the Titan Forest, Ymir completes her regeneration while Eren unsuccessfully attempts to extract information from Reiner and Bertolt. The Warriors discuss their mission, revealing that they intend to capture Historia Reiss due to her connection to the Church of the Walls and the potential link to the 'Coordinate.' As the Survey Corps approaches, signaled by flares, Reiner and Bertolt prepare to flee with their captives. Ymir manipulates the situation by threatening to fight the Warriors unless they allow her to take Historia with them. Upon the arrival of the Survey Corps, Ymir transforms into her Titan form, captures Historia, and escapes by leaping onto the back of the Armored Titan, which Reiner has transformed into to facilitate their retreat. Hannes and the rest of the Survey Corps vow to pursue and rescue Eren.
key_events: Reiner and Bertolt reveal their plan to capture Historia to aid in their mission to find the 'Coordinate'.
The Survey Corps arrives at the Titan Forest, forcing the Warriors to attempt an escape.
Ymir forces Reiner and Bertolt to cooperate with her plan to kidnap Historia.
Ymir transforms into her Titan form and successfully captures Historia.
Reiner transforms into the Armored Titan to carry Ymir, Bertolt, and the captives away from the forest.

summary_text: As the Survey Corps continues their pursuit of the kidnappers, Commander Erwin Smith strategically leads a massive horde of Titans toward the Armored Titan to force a confrontation. Meanwhile, Ymir reveals to Historia that she is defecting to Reiner and Bertolt's side to ensure her own survival, explaining that her Titan powers make her a target and that Historia's status is her only leverage. Historia pledges her loyalty to Ymir regardless of the circumstances. As the soldiers catch up, Mikasa displays a ruthless resolve to kill the traitors, wounding Ymir and ignoring Historia's pleas. Bertolt breaks down emotionally, admitting that while their actions are unforgivable, their time in the 104th Training Corps was genuine. The confrontation is cut short when Erwin arrives with the Titan horde, forcing the soldiers to retreat.
key_events: Erwin Smith uses a horde of Titans as a tactical decoy to intercept the Armored Titan.
Ymir confesses to Historia that she is defecting to protect herself from Reiner and Bertolt's allies.
Historia chooses to side with Ymir, rejecting the safety of the Walls.
Mikasa declares her intent to kill Reiner, Bertolt, and anyone else who stands in her way.
Bertolt experiences an emotional breakdown, claiming his bonds with the 104th Training Corps were real.
The skirmish is interrupted by the arrival of Erwin and the Titan horde.

summary_text: Erwin Smith leads a massive horde of Titans toward the Armored Titan, forcing Reiner and Ymir to defend themselves and allowing the Survey Corps to close in on Eren. Despite losing his right arm to a Titan during the charge, Erwin maintains his command, urging his soldiers forward to rescue Eren. Mikasa attempts to strike Bertolt but is nearly killed before being saved by Jean. Armin manipulates Bertolt by falsely claiming that Annie is being brutally tortured, causing an enraged Bertolt to lose his composure. This distraction allows Erwin to slash Bertolt and successfully retrieve Eren. As the Survey Corps attempts to retreat, Reiner begins throwing Titans at them to impede their escape. The chapter concludes with Eren and Mikasa being knocked from their horse, coming face-to-face with the same Titan that killed Eren's mother five years prior.
key_events: Erwin Smith leads a Titan horde to clash with the Armored Titan.
Erwin Smith loses his right arm to a Titan while ordering the charge.
Armin Arlert psychologically manipulates Bertolt by lying about Annie's torture.
Erwin successfully rescues Eren from Bertolt.
Reiner throws Titans at the retreating Survey Corps.
Eren and Mikasa encounter the Smiling Titan that killed Carla Yeager.

summary_text: As Eren and Mikasa face the Titan that killed Carla, Hannes intervenes to protect them but is ultimately devoured. Overcome by despair and feelings of uselessness, Eren is comforted by Mikasa, who thanks him for his influence on her life. Driven by a surge of rage, Eren attempts to fight the Titan with his bare fists, inadvertently awakening the 'Coordinate'—the Founding Titan's power. This ability commands nearby Titans to swarm and destroy the Smiling Titan. Realizing Eren possesses this power, Reiner and Bertolt attempt to capture him, but Eren commands the Titans to attack the Armored Titan instead, allowing the Survey Corps to retreat. During the chaos, Ymir chooses to abandon the Survey Corps and join Reiner and Bertolt. Later, while resting on Wall Maria, Ymir explains to the duo that she helped them to repay a debt, as she feels a unique kinship with them regarding their shared pasts.
key_events: Hannes is killed and devoured by the Smiling Titan while defending Eren and Mikasa.
Eren awakens the Founding Titan's power (the 'Coordinate') after a moment of intense emotional distress.
Eren commands a horde of Titans to attack the Smiling Titan and subsequently the Armored Titan.
The Survey Corps successfully retreats to the safety of the Walls.
Ymir defects from the Survey Corps to join Reiner and Bertolt, citing a desire to repay her debt to them.

summary_text: Following the rescue operation, the exhausted and decimated Survey Corps returns to the Walls. Eren struggles with guilt over the heavy casualties, but after his comrades suggest he may have the power to control Titans, he regains his resolve to seal Wall Maria and defeat the traitors. Meanwhile, Dot Pixis informs a recovering, amputee Erwin Smith that food supplies are critically low, risking riots if Wall Rose falls. Hange Zoë presents a harrowing theory that Titans are actually humans fused within their bodies, supported by evidence from Ragako Village where the villagers—including Connie Springer's mother—appear to have been transformed into Titans. Levi Ackerman announces the formation of a new Special Operations Squad, consisting of members from the 104th Training Corps, tasked with protecting Eren and Historia Reiss. In an additional scene, Historia discards her 'Krista' persona, revealing it was merely a survival mechanism, and expresses her complex feelings regarding Ymir's departure.
key_events: The Survey Corps returns from the rescue mission with heavy casualties.
Eren resolves to master his Titan-controlling ability to seal Wall Maria.
Dot Pixis warns Erwin of impending food shortages and potential civil unrest.
Hange reveals the theory that Titans are humans transformed and fused with their bodies.
Connie discovers evidence that his mother was turned into a Titan.
Levi establishes a new Special Operations Squad to guard Eren and Historia.
Historia abandons her 'Krista' persona, revealing it was a facade.

summary_text: The new Squad Levi discusses the strategy to retake Wall Maria using Eren's Titan powers, but the mood shifts when Hange reveals that Minister Nick has been murdered. Hange confirms that Nick was tortured to death by the Military Police's First Interior Squad, led by Djel Sannes, signaling a dangerous conspiracy within the Walls. The Survey Corps resolves to proceed with their mission while actively opposing these internal enemies. Meanwhile, Eren reflects on Historia Reiss's past: she was raised in isolation on a Reiss family farm, suffering from maternal neglect and social ostracization. Following the fall of Wall Maria, her father, Rod Reiss, attempted to take her and her mother to live with him, but they were intercepted by Kenny Ackerman and the Interior Squad. Rod denied his paternity to save himself, leading to the murder of Historia's mother. Rod then spared Historia on the condition that she adopt the alias 'Krista Lenz' and live in hiding, eventually leading her to join the 104th Training Corps.
key_events: The new Squad Levi plans to use Eren's powers to seal Wall Maria.
Hange discovers Minister Nick was tortured and murdered by the Military Police's First Interior Squad.
The Survey Corps decides to fight back against the internal conspiracy within the government.
Historia reveals her traumatic childhood and the murder of her mother by Kenny Ackerman's squad.
Rod Reiss forces Historia to adopt the alias 'Krista Lenz' to ensure her survival in hiding.

summary_text: Following a failed experiment to master the hardening ability, Eren Yeager's Titan form becomes increasingly unstable, resulting in incomplete transformations and a dangerous fusion between his human body and the Titan flesh. During these tests, Eren experiences a fragmented memory of a girl named Frieda Reiss. Meanwhile, Erwin Smith confronts Nile Dok regarding the Interior Military Police's extrajudicial murder of Minister Nick, highlighting the corruption within the government. As the Survey Corps prepares to challenge the Royal Government, Levi’s squad relocates to Trost District. They successfully bait the Interior Military Police into kidnapping Jean and Armin, who are disguised as Eren and Historia, allowing the Survey Corps to track the kidnappers and prepare for a counter-strike.
key_events: Eren fails to manifest the hardening ability and shows signs of physical fusion with his Titan form.
Eren experiences a brief, unexplained vision of Frieda Reiss.
Erwin Smith interrogates Nile Dok about the Interior Military Police's autonomy and the murder of Minister Nick.
The Survey Corps executes a decoy operation in Trost District using Jean and Armin as body doubles for Eren and Historia.
Levi and his squad track the kidnappers to their hideout, with Levi confirming he is combat-ready despite his previous injury.

summary_text: Historia Reiss experiences a repressed memory of her childhood, where a mysterious woman—later revealed to be Frieda Reiss—teaches her to be 'ladylike' before magically erasing the memory of their encounter. Upon waking, Historia expresses feelings of emptiness and identity loss, but Eren comforts her by stating he prefers her authentic, 'stupidly honest' self over her previous 'Krista' persona. Meanwhile, the Survey Corps executes a plan to secure the support of the Reeves Company. Levi and his squad ambush Dimo Reeves and his men, eventually convincing Dimo to defect to the Survey Corps' side in exchange for access to luxury goods and a promise of protection. Dimo assists the Corps in staging a fake kidnapping of Eren and Historia, leading to the successful capture of the Military Police officer Djel Sannes. The chapter concludes with Commander Erwin meeting with Dot Pixis to finalize their strategy to overthrow the Royal Government and seize control of the Walls.
key_events: Historia recalls a childhood memory of Frieda Reiss erasing her memories.
Eren validates Historia's true personality, rejecting her former 'Krista' persona.
The Survey Corps ambushes and recruits Dimo Reeves to their cause.
The Survey Corps successfully captures the Military Police officer Djel Sannes for interrogation.
Erwin Smith and Dot Pixis agree to initiate a coup d'état against the Royal Government.

summary_text: Erwin Smith meets with Commander Dot Pixis to propose a coup d'état against the Royal Government. Erwin reveals his motivation stems from his father's murder by the government after he discovered the truth about the Walls and the outside world. Meanwhile, Hange and Levi brutally interrogate the captured Interior Squad member Djel Sannes. By tricking Sannes into believing his comrade Ralph has betrayed him, they break his resolve. Sannes confesses that the Reiss family is the true royal bloodline. Upon receiving this intelligence, Erwin finalizes his plan to overthrow the current regime and install Historia Reiss as the rightful Queen. Elsewhere, the younger Survey Corps members grapple with the moral decay required to survive, with Armin acknowledging that they are no longer 'good people' as they prepare to commit crimes for the sake of humanity.
key_events: Erwin Smith presents his plan to overthrow the Royal Government to Dot Pixis.
Erwin recounts his father's death at the hands of the government for questioning the history of the Walls.
Hange and Levi torture Djel Sannes for information regarding the monarchy.
Sannes reveals that the Reiss family is the true royal bloodline.
Erwin decides to install Historia Reiss as the new Queen to legitimize the coup.

summary_text: Eren records his memories of Reiner, Bertolt, and Ymir, while Hange reveals to Djel Sannes that he was tricked into confessing through a staged interrogation. Meanwhile, Levi informs his squad and the Reeves Company that Historia Reiss is the true heir to the throne. Despite her initial reluctance, Levi pressures Historia into accepting the role of queen to help overthrow the current government. Erwin Smith’s plan is set in motion: Dimo Reeves is to hand over Eren and Historia to the Military Police Interior Squad to lure out Rod Reiss. However, the operation goes awry when Kenny Ackerman appears, kills Dimo Reeves, and captures Eren and Historia, revealing his past connection to Levi and his role in the murder of Historia's mother.
key_events: Hange Zoë tricks Djel Sannes into revealing information about the monarchy.
Levi Ackerman pressures Historia Reiss into accepting her role as the true queen.
Erwin Smith initiates a plan to use Eren and Historia as bait to locate Rod Reiss.
Kenny Ackerman kills Dimo Reeves and kidnaps Eren and Historia.
Kenny Ackerman reveals his identity to Historia and his past as Levi's mentor.

summary_text: Hange and Erwin deduce that Titans are humans seeking to regain their humanity by consuming Titan shifters, leading them to conclude the Reiss family intends to feed Eren to a Titan. As the government frames Erwin for the murder of Dimo Reeves, he appoints Hange as his successor before surrendering to the Military Police. Meanwhile, in Stohess District, Levi tracks Eren and Historia to the Military Police, only to be ambushed by his former mentor, Kenny Ackerman, who initiates a lethal confrontation by killing members of Levi's reconnaissance team.
key_events: Hange theorizes that Titans are humans attempting to regain their form by consuming Titan shifters.
Erwin appoints Hange as the next Commander of the Survey Corps before turning himself in to the Military Police.
Flegel Reeves provides Hange with information regarding the abduction of Eren and Historia.
Levi identifies his former mentor, Kenny Ackerman, as the leader of the anti-personnel squad.
Kenny Ackerman ambushes Levi's team in Stohess, killing Nifa and other soldiers.

summary_text: Levi Ackerman engages in a tactical skirmish with Kenny Ackerman and his Anti-Personnel Control Squad. After being ambushed, Levi retreats into a saloon, where he engages in a psychological exchange with Kenny regarding their shared past in the Underground. Levi manages to turn the tables by using a shotgun to shoot Kenny in the chest, allowing him to escape while killing several pursuers. Levi reunites with his squad, abandoning their original plan to track Eren and Historia, and orders his team to use lethal force against the enemy. During the ensuing chaos, Jean Kirstein hesitates to kill an enemy soldier, resulting in a gunshot being fired and the successful abduction of Eren and Historia. The chapter concludes with Eren and Historia being brought before Rod Reiss, who surprises Eren by embracing Historia and apologizing for his past actions.
key_events: Levi engages in a tactical duel with Kenny Ackerman and escapes into a saloon.
Levi shoots Kenny in the chest using a saloon owner's shotgun and kills several pursuing soldiers.
Levi reunites with his squad and orders them to kill any enemy soldiers to facilitate their escape.
Jean Kirstein hesitates to kill an enemy soldier, leading to a gunshot and the successful kidnapping of Eren and Historia.
Eren and Historia are brought to Rod Reiss, who unexpectedly embraces Historia and offers an apology.

summary_text: In the aftermath of the Stohess District battle, Commander Nile Dok is pressured by the press to ignore the activities of the Military Police's First Interior Squad, leaving him fearful that their new anti-personnel gear may eventually be turned against him. Meanwhile, Armin Arlert struggles with the trauma of killing a soldier to save Jean Kirstein, though Levi and his comrades reassure him that his actions were necessary for their survival. The Survey Corps captures two Military Police scouts, Hitch Dreyse and Marlowe Freudenberg, who were patrolling the forest. After revealing the truth about Annie Leonhart’s identity as the Female Titan, Jean tests the pair's loyalty by staging a mock execution. Convinced of their sincerity and shared desire for a better military, Jean recruits them to the cause. With intelligence provided by Marlowe, the Survey Corps locates the Interior Brigade Compound, where they believe Eren and Historia are being held, and prepare to launch a rescue mission.
key_events: Nile Dok is coerced by the press to suppress information regarding the First Interior Squad.
Armin grapples with the psychological weight of taking a human life for the first time.
The Survey Corps captures Hitch Dreyse and Marlowe Freudenberg.
The Survey Corps reveals to Hitch that Annie Leonhart was the Female Titan.
Jean tests Marlowe and Hitch's loyalty through a staged confrontation, ultimately recruiting them.
The group uses intelligence from Marlowe to locate the Interior Brigade Compound.

summary_text: Levi Ackerman interrogates a captured member of the Interior Military Police, learning that the soldier is unaware of Eren and Historia's location but revealing that Kenny Ackerman is his superior. Meanwhile, Hange Zoë and Moblit Berner recruit journalists Roy and Peaure to expose the Royal Government's corruption. In Trost District, Hange and Moblit rescue Flegel Reeves from an assassination attempt by the Military Police, using the testimony of local citizens to publicly implicate the Interior Squad in Dimo Reeves' murder. Despite this, the journalists fear printing the truth due to threats against their families. The chapter concludes in the capital, where a battered Erwin Smith is brought before King Fritz and the Royal Government Assembly to face judgment.
key_events: Levi interrogates an Interior MP and discovers Kenny Ackerman's surname.
Hange and Moblit recruit journalists to document the Survey Corps' struggle against the government.
Flegel Reeves is saved from an assassination attempt by Hange and Moblit in Trost.
Trost citizens witness the Military Police's confession regarding Dimo Reeves' death.
Flegel Reeves assumes leadership of the Reeves Company.
Erwin Smith is presented before the King and the Royal Government Assembly.

summary_text: Erwin Smith faces the Royal Government Assembly, arguing that the Survey Corps is essential for humanity's survival and warning of the civil unrest that would follow a breach of Wall Rose. During the hearing, a Garrison soldier reports a Titan breach at Wall Rose. When the Assembly orders the gates of Wall Sheena sealed to prevent refugees from entering—effectively condemning half the population to death—Nile Dok defies the order. It is then revealed that the breach report was a ruse orchestrated by Dot Pixis to expose the nobility's selfishness. Darius Zackly arrives to arrest the corrupt officials, successfully executing a military coup. Meanwhile, Hange informs Levi’s squad that they have been exonerated, though Levi notes the mission is incomplete as Kenny Ackerman still holds Eren and Historia captive. Using intelligence from a letter, the squad prepares to track down the hostages, while at a remote chapel, Rod Reiss and Kenny keep Eren and Historia in chains.
key_events: Erwin Smith defends the necessity of the Survey Corps before the Royal Assembly.
Dot Pixis and Anka stage a fake report of a Titan breach to test the nobility's morality.
The Royal Assembly chooses to sacrifice refugees to protect their own interests, confirming their corruption.
Darius Zackly and the military arrest the Royal Government, successfully completing the coup.
Levi’s squad is officially exonerated and begins planning a rescue mission for Eren and Historia.

summary_text: Following the successful coup by the Survey Corps, Erwin Smith and Darius Zackly discuss the moral weight of their actions and their motivations for overthrowing the monarchy. Meanwhile, Hange Zoë and Squad Levi investigate the Reiss family, uncovering a history of tragedy and suspecting that Eren Yeager is being held at the Reiss chapel to be consumed by a Titan to transfer his powers. In a subterranean cavern, Rod Reiss and Historia confront a captive Eren. By touching Eren, Rod triggers a series of repressed memories, revealing that five years ago, Grisha Yeager slaughtered the Reiss family and subsequently injected Eren with Titan serum. The vision concludes with the revelation that Eren transformed into a Titan and consumed his own father, Grisha, to inherit his power.
key_events: Erwin Smith and Darius Zackly publicly announce the fall of the corrupt Royal Government.
Hange Zoë deduces that the Reiss family was likely killed by a Titan and that Eren is being held at the Reiss chapel to have his powers stolen.
Rod Reiss and Historia Reiss confront Eren in a crystal cavern.
Rod and Historia touch Eren, triggering a flashback of the night Wall Maria fell.
Eren witnesses the memory of Grisha Yeager slaughtering the Reiss family and injecting Eren with Titan serum.
The memory reveals that Eren transformed into a Titan and ate his father, Grisha, to gain his current abilities.

summary_text: In the cavern beneath the Reiss Chapel, Rod Reiss reveals to Historia that her half-sister, Frieda, was killed by Grisha Yeager five years ago when he stole the Founding Titan. Rod explains that Frieda's memories live on and offers Historia a Titan injection, urging her to consume Eren to reclaim those memories and the family power. Meanwhile, the Survey Corps, led by Erwin Smith, prepares to storm the chapel to rescue Eren. Elsewhere, Levi discusses the Ackerman bloodline with Mikasa, noting that both he and Kenny experienced a sudden, inexplicable surge of power in their youth, which Mikasa confirms having felt during her childhood kidnapping. As the military forces mobilize, Eren, realizing he is about to suffer the same fate as the Reiss family, struggles against his chains.
key_events: Rod Reiss reveals the history of Frieda Reiss and her death at the hands of Grisha Yeager.
Erwin Smith and the military leadership finalize plans to siege the Reiss Chapel.
Levi and Mikasa discuss the mysterious 'awakened' power inherent to the Ackerman clan.
Rod Reiss prepares a Titan injection for Historia, pressuring her to eat Eren to inherit the Founding Titan.
Eren realizes the danger of his situation and attempts to break free from his restraints.

summary_text: Squad Levi launches a tactical assault on the Reiss family chapel to rescue Eren and Historia. Using oil-filled barrels and smoke to blind the Anti-Personnel Control Squad, the Survey Corps exploits the blind spots of their enemies' vertical maneuvering equipment to gain the upper hand. During the skirmish, Hange Zoë is seriously injured by Traute Caven, forcing the squad to regroup. Meanwhile, Rod Reiss explains the history of the Walls to Historia and Eren, revealing that the Founding Titan was used by the Reiss family to alter human memories for a century. He explains that the power must be passed down through consumption, but notes that Eren cannot utilize the Titan's full potential because he lacks royal blood. As the chapter concludes, Kenny Ackerman confronts Rod, revealing his own ambition to consume Eren and inherit the Titan's power for himself.
key_events: Squad Levi infiltrates the Reiss chapel using a smoke-and-fire diversion.
The Survey Corps successfully exploits the tactical weaknesses of the Anti-Personnel Control Squad.
Hange Zoë is critically wounded by Traute Caven during the battle.
Rod Reiss reveals the history of the Founding Titan and the Reiss family's ritual of passing power through consumption.
Rod confirms that Eren cannot use the Founding Titan's power because he is not of royal blood.
Kenny Ackerman reveals his intention to turn into a Titan by eating Eren.

summary_text: Kenny Ackerman recounts his grandfather's history regarding the Ackerman family's persecution, revealing that they and the Asian clan are immune to the King's memory manipulation, which is why they were targeted. Kenny confronts Rod Reiss, exposing his hypocrisy and revealing that Rod only sought Historia out to use her as a vessel to inherit the Founding Titan. As the situation escalates, Kenny releases Eren, hoping to force a confrontation, but Eren, overwhelmed by despair, refuses to transform and instead begs Historia to eat him to save humanity.
key_events: Kenny reveals the lore that the Ackerman and Asian bloodlines are immune to the King's memory manipulation.
Kenny attacks Rod Reiss, accusing him of being a coward who only values Historia for her royal blood.
Kenny frees Eren, attempting to incite a fight between Eren and Historia.
Eren refuses to transform into a Titan and tearfully pleads with Historia to consume him.

summary_text: As Historia Reiss prepares to inherit the Founding Titan by consuming Eren Yeager, she experiences flashbacks of Ymir, Frieda Reiss, and her own past. Rod Reiss reveals the history of the Reiss family, explaining that the First King’s ideology—which prioritizes the world being ruled by Titans—is inherited by those who possess the Founding Titan. Rejecting her father's manipulation and the burden of becoming a 'god,' Historia shatters the Titan serum vial and rescues Eren. Enraged and desperate, Rod Reiss consumes the spilled serum, transforming into a massive, skeletal Titan that dwarfs the Colossus Titan. As the cavern collapses, Levi’s squad rescues Eren. Faced with the impending threat and his own feelings of inadequacy, Eren chooses to trust his friends and himself. He consumes a vial labeled 'Armor' and transforms into a new Titan form to confront Rod Reiss.
key_events: Historia rejects Rod Reiss's plan and breaks the Titan serum vial.
Rod Reiss consumes the serum and transforms into a gargantuan, skeletal Titan.
Historia frees Eren from his chains, declaring herself an enemy of humanity but a friend to Eren.
Levi’s squad successfully extracts Eren from the collapsing chapel.
Eren consumes a vial labeled 'Armor' and transforms into a new Titan form to face Rod Reiss.

summary_text: Following the collapse of the Reiss chapel, Eren is extracted from his hardened Titan form, which remains intact and supports the cavern ceiling. The Survey Corps discovers that Rod Reiss has transformed into a massive, crawling Titan—significantly larger than the Colossus Titan—that is moving toward the Orvud District. Hange theorizes that the Titan is drawn to densely populated areas, meaning it will eventually threaten the interior of Wall Sheena if not stopped. While the group initially debates whether to feed Eren to Rod to restore his humanity and potentially reclaim the Founding Titan's power, Historia rejects this, arguing that the Reiss family's ideology is a trap and that humanity must find a way to survive without their bloodline. Eren resolves to kill his father's killer, and Historia accepts that she must participate in the battle to confront her destiny. Commander Erwin Smith orchestrates a plan to use the Orvud District citizens as a decoy to lure the Titan to the Wall, where the Survey Corps prepares for a final confrontation at dawn.
key_events: Eren is extracted from his hardened Titan form, which successfully prevented the cave from collapsing.
Rod Reiss transforms into a gargantuan, crawling Abnormal Titan that begins moving toward the Orvud District.
The Survey Corps decides to abandon the plan of restoring Rod Reiss and instead resolves to kill him.
Historia Reiss rejects her father's ideology and commits to fighting alongside the Survey Corps.
Erwin Smith orders the Orvud District to remain populated to act as a lure for the Titan, planning to intercept and kill it at the Wall.

summary_text: As Rod Reiss’s massive Titan approaches Orvud District, the Garrison’s artillery proves ineffective. Despite Erwin Smith’s orders for her to remain safe, Historia Reiss insists on joining the fight, determined to fulfill her duty. Eren Yeager, struggling with feelings of inadequacy, transforms into his Titan form to assist Squad Levi. Following a plan devised by Erwin, the squad uses gunpowder-laden wheelbarrows to destroy the Titan's hands, causing him to lose his balance. Eren then forces gunpowder barrels into Rod’s mouth, blowing his head apart. Squad Levi and Historia intercept the flying debris, with Historia successfully locating and severing the nape. Upon killing her father, she experiences a series of memories regarding the Reiss family's history and the burden of the Founding Titan. After the Titan body disintegrates, Historia emerges before the terrified citizens of Orvud and publicly declares herself as Historia Reiss, the true ruler of the Walls.
key_events: Historia Reiss defies Erwin Smith's orders to stay behind and joins the front lines.
Eren Yeager transforms into his Titan form to execute the plan against Rod Reiss.
Squad Levi and Eren use gunpowder to destroy the hands and head of Rod Reiss's Titan.
Historia delivers the killing blow to Rod Reiss and experiences ancestral memories.
Historia publicly reveals her identity as the true queen to the citizens of Orvud District.

summary_text: As he lies dying from injuries sustained during the collapse of the Reiss Chapel, Kenny Ackerman reflects on his life and his complex relationship with Uri Reiss. Years prior, Kenny attempted to assassinate Uri, but the King spared him, apologized for the monarchy's persecution of the Ackermans, and earned Kenny's loyalty as his bodyguard. During this time, Kenny discovered his sister Kuchel had died in the Underground and took in her young son, Levi, teaching him survival skills before eventually abandoning him. Kenny later sought the power of the Founding Titan, forming the Anti-Personnel Control Squad to pursue his ambitions. In his final moments, Kenny reunites with Levi, reveals he is his uncle, and bequeaths him a stolen Titan injection before passing away. Following these events, Historia Reiss is crowned Queen of the Walls. She fulfills a challenge to punch Levi, who responds with a rare smile, thanking his comrades.
key_events: Kenny Ackerman recalls his past, including his failed assassination attempt on Uri Reiss and their subsequent friendship.
Kenny recounts raising and eventually abandoning his nephew, Levi, in the Underground.
Kenny reveals his motivation for forming the Anti-Personnel Control Squad was to obtain the Founding Titan's power.
Kenny dies from his wounds after revealing his familial connection to Levi and gifting him the stolen Titan serum.
Historia Reiss is officially crowned Queen of the Walls.
Historia punches Levi as part of a challenge, and Levi reacts with a rare smile of gratitude toward his team.

summary_text: Two months after the military coup, the Survey Corps has consolidated power, with Historia Reiss acting as a figurehead queen while managing an orphanage for the needy. Eren Yeager has made significant progress in mastering his Titan hardening abilities, which have been utilized to seal the hole in Trost District and construct a guillotine-like weapon on the Walls to execute Titans. Despite these advancements, the physical toll of hardening on Eren remains a concern. The military leadership, including Erwin Smith and Levi Ackerman, prepares for the upcoming expedition to retake Wall Maria, while the mysterious Titan serum recovered from Kenny Ackerman is entrusted to Levi. Meanwhile, new recruits—including Marlowe Freudenberg—join the Survey Corps, though they clash with veterans over their naive enthusiasm. Eren, prompted by memories of his father Grisha meeting their former instructor Keith Shadis, resolves to confront Shadis to unlock further secrets about his past. In Shiganshina, the Beast Titan defeats the Armored Titan; the human inside the Beast Titan, Zeke, orders Reiner and Bertolt to wait for the Survey Corps to arrive so they can ambush them and seize the Founding Titan.
key_events: Historia Reiss establishes an orphanage using confiscated royal assets.
Eren masters Titan hardening, enabling the creation of a Titan-killing guillotine weapon.
Military leadership entrusts the mysterious Titan injection serum to Levi.
Marlowe Freudenberg and other recruits join the Survey Corps.
Eren identifies Keith Shadis as the man in his father's memories and plans to confront him.
The Beast Titan defeats the Armored Titan in Shiganshina and prepares an ambush for the Survey Corps.

summary_text: Eren Yeager and his comrades visit former Survey Corps Commander Keith Shadis to uncover the truth about Eren's father, Grisha Yeager. Through a series of flashbacks, Keith reveals that he discovered an amnesiac Grisha outside Wall Maria twenty years ago and helped him integrate into society. Keith recounts his own tenure as Commander, his rivalry with the rising star Erwin Smith, and his complicated relationship with Grisha and Carla Yeager. He details the day Wall Maria fell, during which he witnessed Grisha taking a young Eren into the forest, followed by a mysterious flash of light and the subsequent disappearance of Grisha, leaving only an unconscious Eren behind. Keith admits he sabotaged Eren's training equipment years later to prevent him from joining the military, hoping to save him from a dangerous life, but was moved by Eren's persistence. Ultimately, Keith reflects on his life as a 'bystander' who lacked the greatness to change the course of history, while Eren comes to terms with his father's legacy and his own existence.
key_events: Keith Shadis recounts his first meeting with Grisha Yeager outside the Walls.
Keith details his time as Survey Corps Commander and his eventual resignation in favor of Erwin Smith.
Keith describes the fall of Shiganshina and his final encounter with Grisha, who took Eren into the forest.
Keith reveals he sabotaged Eren's vertical maneuvering equipment during training to keep him out of the military.
Eren and Keith both reflect on the realization that Eren's value is inherent to his birth, rather than his status as a 'special' person.

summary_text: As the Survey Corps prepares for the expedition to retake Wall Maria, Hange and Erwin analyze the revelations regarding Grisha Yeager, concluding that he likely possessed knowledge of the outside world and the Reiss family that he was unable to share. Levi attempts to convince an injured Erwin to stay behind, but Erwin insists on participating, driven by his need to witness the truth about the world. Meanwhile, the soldiers enjoy a rare meal of meat, which leads to a chaotic brawl involving a food-crazed Sasha, followed by a heated argument between Eren and Jean regarding their roles and survival. As the tension settles, Eren, Mikasa, and Armin reaffirm their bond and their shared dream of seeing the wonders of the outside world, such as the sea. The chapter concludes with the Survey Corps receiving a rare, supportive send-off from the citizens of the Walls, while Reiner and Bertolt wait in Shiganshina for their arrival.
key_events: Erwin and Hange discuss Grisha Yeager's secret knowledge and the importance of the basement.
Levi confronts Erwin about his participation in the mission, eventually accepting Erwin's resolve to see the truth.
A chaotic fight breaks out among the soldiers over a rare meat dinner, requiring Levi to intervene.
Eren, Mikasa, and Armin discuss their future and the dream of seeing the sea after the mission.
The Survey Corps departs for the mission to retake Wall Maria with public support.
Reiner and Bertolt are shown waiting in Shiganshina for the Survey Corps' arrival.

summary_text: The Survey Corps launches their operation to retake Wall Maria, traveling under the cover of darkness to reach the Shiganshina District. Eren Yeager initially struggles with anxiety regarding the weight of the mission, but regains his resolve after a conversation with Armin Arlert about their shared desire for freedom. Upon arriving at Shiganshina, Erwin Smith orders the soldiers to use vertical maneuvering equipment and deploy in hooded groups to confuse the enemy regarding Eren's location. As the team enters the ruined district, Armin discovers a campfire, signaling that Reiner Braun and Bertolt Hoover are nearby. Despite Hange Zoë and Levi Ackerman noting the suspicious absence of Titans, the Survey Corps proceeds with the mission. Eren prepares to transform into a Titan to seal the hole in the wall, while Reiner and Bertolt watch from a hidden vantage point.
key_events: The Survey Corps reaches the Shiganshina District under the cover of night.
Eren overcomes his fear of failure through a heart-to-heart with Armin.
Erwin implements a decoy strategy, having 100 soldiers wear hoods to mask Eren's identity.
Armin discovers a campfire, confirming that Reiner and Bertolt are lying in wait.
Eren prepares to transform into a Titan to seal the breach in Wall Maria.

summary_text: Eren successfully seals the hole in Wall Maria using his hardening ability, but the Survey Corps realizes their mission is far from over as Reiner and Bertolt remain at large. Armin discovers evidence of a recent campsite, leading him to deduce that the enemy was monitoring their approach. Suspecting that the enemies are hiding in an unconventional location, Armin orders a search inside the Wall itself. His hunch proves correct when they locate Reiner hiding within a hollow section of the structure. Levi manages to ambush and severely wound Reiner, but fails to secure a kill before Reiner transforms into the Armored Titan. Simultaneously, the Beast Titan appears with an army of Titans and destroys the northern gate, trapping the Survey Corps inside Shiganshina and cutting off their escape route. Erwin prepares the Corps for a desperate, final stand against the enemy forces.
key_events: Eren seals the hole in Wall Maria using his Titan hardening ability.
Armin discovers a recently used campsite, leading to the realization that the enemy had advance warning of the Corps' arrival.
Armin deduces that Reiner and Bertolt are hiding inside the Wall itself.
The Survey Corps locates Reiner, and Levi critically wounds him before he transforms into the Armored Titan.
The Beast Titan appears and blocks the northern gate with a boulder, trapping the Survey Corps inside the district without their horses.

summary_text: The Survey Corps launches their operation to retake Wall Maria, only to find themselves immediately cornered by the Beast Titan's strategic blockade. As the Beast Titan commands his forces to target the Corps' horses to cut off their retreat and supply lines, Commander Erwin Smith orchestrates a counter-strategy. He identifies a quadrupedal intelligent Titan acting as a scout and realizes the enemy's intent to starve them out. Erwin splits his forces, tasking Levi with hunting the Beast Titan while he uses Eren as bait to lure the Armored Titan away from the horses. Reiner, realizing he is being manipulated, chooses to pursue Eren into Shiganshina District, leading to a direct confrontation between the two Titan shifters.
key_events: The Beast Titan and his army initiate a blockade of Shiganshina, targeting the Survey Corps' horses.
Erwin identifies a quadrupedal Titan as an intelligent scout for the enemy forces.
Erwin assigns Levi to kill the Beast Titan while ordering other squads to protect the horses.
Eren transforms into a Titan to act as bait, successfully luring Reiner away from the horses and into the district.
Reiner realizes Erwin's tactical trap but decides to engage Eren to prevent him from escaping and mastering the Coordinate.

summary_text: During the battle for Shiganshina, Levi coordinates the Survey Corps recruits while Erwin Smith struggles with the temptation to abandon his command to reach the Yeager basement and uncover the truth about the world. On the battlefield, Eren uses his hardening abilities to fight the Armored Titan, but Reiner gains the upper hand. Hange Zoë’s squad intervenes, utilizing their new weapon, the 'Thunder Spears,' to blind Reiner and destroy his armor. Despite hesitation from Sasha and Connie, Jean Kirstein rallies the squad to deliver a final, explosive strike to Reiner's nape, leaving the Armored Titan incapacitated.
key_events: Erwin Smith reflects on his past and contemplates abandoning his soldiers to reach the basement.
Hange Zoë introduces the Thunder Spears, a new weapon designed to penetrate the Armored Titan's armor.
Mikasa and Hange blind the Armored Titan by firing Thunder Spears into its eyes.
The Survey Corps squad uses the Thunder Spears to strip away the Armored Titan's armor and expose its nape.
Jean Kirstein convinces a hesitant Sasha and Connie to participate in the final assault on Reiner.

summary_text: The chapter alternates between a flashback to the Trost District battle and the present-day conflict in Shiganshina. In the flashback, Reiner and Bertolt are confronted by Marco Bott after he overhears them discussing their Titan identities. Reiner forces Annie to remove Marco's vertical maneuvering equipment, leaving him to be devoured by a Titan while the three watch in horror. In the present, Zeke Yeager rallies Reiner and Bertolt to secure the Founding Titan and end their 'cursed history.' As the Survey Corps celebrates the apparent defeat of the Armored Titan, Reiner lets out a roar, signaling Zeke to launch a barrel containing Bertolt into the district. The Survey Corps realizes too late that Bertolt is about to transform and unleash the Colossus Titan upon them.
key_events: Flashback reveals Reiner, Bertolt, and Annie were responsible for Marco Bott's death in Trost.
Zeke Yeager threatens to replace Reiner as the Armored Titan if he fails to secure the Founding Titan.
Reiner and Bertolt prepare for the final battle, agreeing to act independently.
The Survey Corps believes they have killed Reiner, but his Titan body emits a roar.
Zeke throws a barrel containing Bertolt into Shiganshina, signaling the start of the Colossus Titan's assault.

summary_text: As the Survey Corps prepares for the Colossus Titan's arrival, Bertolt Hoover aborts his initial transformation to rescue a mangled Reiner Braun, who survived by transferring his consciousness to his nervous system. Armin Arlert attempts to negotiate with Bertolt, but Bertolt remains resolute in his mission to destroy humanity within the Walls, having moved past his previous emotional hesitation. After a brief skirmish with Mikasa Ackerman, Bertolt retreats and prepares to transform. Meanwhile, the Survey Corps' attempt to execute Reiner is thwarted when his Titan form shifts to protect his human body. Bertolt ultimately transforms into the Colossus Titan, creating a massive explosion that engulfs Hange Zoë's squad and leaves only Squad Levi to face the threat.
key_events: Bertolt discovers Reiner is alive and decides to prioritize his comrade's safety over an immediate attack.
Armin attempts to manipulate Bertolt through negotiation, but Bertolt reveals he has seen through the ruse.
Reiner's Titan form shifts its position to prevent the Survey Corps from reaching his human body.
Bertolt transforms into the Colossus Titan, causing a massive explosion that kills Hange's squad.
Squad Levi is left as the only remaining force to confront the Colossus Titan.

summary_text: Bertolt Hoover, in his Colossus Titan form, begins a systematic destruction of Shiganshina District. While the Survey Corps struggles to formulate a strategy, Armin Arlert defers command to Jean Kirstein, who leads the squad in a desperate attempt to stop Bertolt. On the other side of Wall Maria, the Beast Titan (Zeke Yeager) launches a devastating long-range artillery attack using rocks, annihilating the squads led by Dirk, Marlene, and Klaus. Levi Ackerman is forced to retreat to the base of the Wall, where he reunites with Erwin Smith and the remaining recruits. Meanwhile, Eren Yeager attempts to physically restrain the Colossus Titan to draw its attention, but Bertolt easily counters the attack, kicking Eren into the Wall and leaving him incapacitated.
key_events: Bertolt Hoover begins destroying Shiganshina District as the Colossus Titan.
Jean Kirstein assumes tactical command of the squad after Armin struggles to find a solution.
Zeke Yeager, aided by the Cart Titan, uses rock projectiles to slaughter the Survey Corps' remaining squads.
Eren Yeager attempts to physically stop the Colossus Titan but is kicked into the Wall and incapacitated.
Erwin Smith and Levi Ackerman assess the dire situation as the Survey Corps faces total annihilation.

summary_text: Following the Colossus Titan's assault, the Survey Corps struggles to counter Bertolt, who uses a burst of steam to deflect Mikasa's Thunder Spears and injure the squad. As the Armored Titan recovers, the situation grows dire. Erwin Smith, pressured by Levi to retreat, reveals a final, desperate strategy: a suicidal frontal charge against the Beast Titan. Erwin confesses his lifelong desire to reach the basement to uncover the truth about his father's research, but ultimately chooses to abandon this dream to lead the recruits into a sacrificial decoy maneuver. While Erwin and the new recruits charge Zeke, using signal flares to obscure his vision, Levi maneuvers through the surrounding Titans to launch a surprise attack. The chapter concludes with Erwin and his soldiers being decimated by Zeke's rock barrage, even as Erwin continues to rally them to fight until the end.
key_events: Bertolt deflects a coordinated attack by Mikasa and the Special Operations Squad using a burst of steam.
The Armored Titan fully recovers and prepares to rejoin the battle.
Erwin Smith reveals his true motivation for joining the Survey Corps: to discover the secrets of the basement.
Levi convinces Erwin to abandon his personal dreams and lead the recruits in a suicide charge.
The Survey Corps launches a frontal assault on the Beast Titan to serve as a distraction for Levi.
Erwin and the majority of the recruits are killed by the Beast Titan's rock projectiles.

summary_text: The Survey Corps suffers heavy casualties from the Beast Titan's rock barrages, resulting in the near-death of Erwin Smith and the deaths of Marlowe Freudenberg and other recruits. Levi Ackerman successfully ambushes Zeke Yeager, systematically dismantling the Beast Titan and extracting Zeke from its nape. Levi intends to feed Zeke to a surviving soldier using a Titan injection to steal the Beast Titan's power, but the Cart Titan intervenes, rescuing Zeke and fleeing. Meanwhile, on the other side of the Wall, Armin Arlert deduces that the Colossus Titan's energy is finite and can be exhausted through a prolonged battle. He coordinates a plan for Jean and Mikasa to distract the Armored Titan while he and Eren take on Bertolt. Armin successfully rouses a dormant Eren, reminding him of their shared dream to see the ocean to motivate him for the final confrontation.
key_events: Erwin Smith is critically wounded by a boulder thrown by the Beast Titan.
Levi Ackerman launches a surprise attack, defeating the Beast Titan and capturing Zeke Yeager.
The Cart Titan rescues Zeke from Levi before he can be fed to a survivor.
Armin Arlert formulates a strategy to defeat the Colossus Titan by exploiting its energy consumption.
Armin wakes Eren Yeager from his Titan form to initiate the final assault against Bertolt.

summary_text: In the Shiganshina District, Armin Arlert executes a desperate plan to defeat the Colossus Titan by acting as a decoy. While Mikasa Ackerman, Jean Kirstein, Connie Springer, and Sasha Blouse engage the Armored Titan, Mikasa successfully cripples Reiner Braun with a Thunder Spear. Reiner, suffering from memory loss, struggles to defend himself. Meanwhile, Armin grapples onto the Colossus Titan, intentionally enduring the intense heat of Bertolt Hoover's steam to keep him distracted. As Armin is burned alive, he entrusts his dream of seeing the ocean to Eren Yeager. With Bertolt focused on the dying Armin, he fails to notice that the Titan form he is attacking is merely a hardened shell. Eren, having flanked the Colossus Titan, strikes from behind and successfully extracts Bertolt from his nape. The chapter concludes with Eren standing over the scorched body of his friend, mourning Armin as the bravest of their comrades.
key_events: Mikasa cripples the Armored Titan with a Thunder Spear.
Armin sacrifices himself by enduring the Colossus Titan's steam to serve as a distraction.
Hange Zoë assists the recruits in forcing open the Armored Titan's jaw, allowing Mikasa to defeat Reiner.
Eren reveals his Titan form was a hardened shell and successfully extracts Bertolt from the Colossus Titan.
Armin is left severely burned and incapacitated, leading Eren to reflect on their shared dream of the ocean.

summary_text: Following the battle in Shiganshina, Eren discovers that Armin is still alive despite his severe burns. Zeke Yeager appears, claiming that he and Eren are both victims of Grisha Yeager's brainwashing, but he is forced to retreat upon the arrival of Levi Ackerman. Meanwhile, Hange attempts to interrogate a captured Reiner, but Zeke and the Cart Titan intervene, rescuing Reiner and escaping. As Eren prepares to use the Titan serum to save Armin by having him consume Bertolt, a recruit arrives carrying a dying Erwin Smith. Levi decides to prioritize saving Erwin over Armin, leading to a tense confrontation as the remaining Survey Corps members clash over who should receive the life-saving injection.
key_events: Zeke Yeager confronts Eren and claims they are both victims of Grisha Yeager.
Levi Ackerman forces Zeke to retreat, preventing him from rescuing Bertolt.
Zeke and the Cart Titan successfully rescue Reiner Braun from the Survey Corps.
Eren pleads with Levi to use the Titan serum to save Armin's life.
Floch Forster arrives with a critically injured Erwin Smith, causing Levi to reconsider his decision.
Levi declares his intent to save Erwin instead of Armin, leading to a standoff with Eren and Mikasa.

summary_text: Following the battle in Shiganshina, Levi Ackerman, Eren Yeager, and Mikasa Ackerman engage in a heated conflict over who should receive the Titan serum to be revived: the dying Commander Erwin Smith or the dying Armin Arlert. Floch Forster, the sole survivor of the suicide charge, insists that Erwin must be saved as a 'demon' necessary for humanity's survival. Hange Zoë and the rest of the squad arrive to restrain a hysterical Mikasa. Levi ultimately chooses to save Armin after reflecting on Armin's dream of seeing the ocean and realizing that Erwin deserves to finally rest from his burdens rather than be dragged back into their hellish reality. Consequently, Armin is injected with the serum, transforms into a pure Titan, and devours the captured Bertolt Hoover, thereby inheriting the power of the Colossus Titan. Erwin Smith passes away from his injuries, and the squad reunites with a resurrected Armin.
key_events: Levi, Eren, and Mikasa clash physically and verbally over the use of the Titan serum.
Floch Forster argues that Erwin Smith is essential for humanity's survival and must be revived.
Levi decides to spare Erwin from further suffering and chooses to save Armin instead.
Armin Arlert is injected with the serum and transforms into a pure Titan.
Bertolt Hoover is eaten by the mindless Titan Armin, resulting in Armin inheriting the Colossus Titan.
Erwin Smith dies from his wounds.

summary_text: Armin Arlert awakens to learn that he has inherited the Colossus Titan after consuming Bertolt Hoover, a decision made by Levi Ackerman after a heated conflict with Eren and Mikasa. Hange Zoë assumes the role of Survey Corps commander and informs Armin that he must now carry the burden of Erwin Smith's legacy. Eren, Mikasa, Levi, and Hange travel to Eren's childhood home in Shiganshina to finally unlock the basement. After breaking down the door, they discover a hidden compartment in Grisha Yeager's desk containing three journals. Inside one, they find a photograph of Grisha with a woman and a child, accompanied by a note revealing that humanity outside the Walls has not gone extinct and lives in a state of advanced civilization.
key_events: Armin learns he has become a Titan shifter and consumed Bertolt.
Hange Zoë officially succeeds Erwin Smith as the commander of the Survey Corps.
Eren, Mikasa, Levi, and Hange successfully enter the basement of the Yeager household.
The group discovers a photograph and journals proving that humanity exists and thrives outside the Walls.

summary_text: Grisha Yeager recounts his childhood in the Liberio internment zone, where he and his sister Faye disobeyed orders to leave the zone to watch a blimp. They were caught by Marleyan soldiers; while Grisha was disciplined, Faye was taken away and later found dead. Grisha's father, a doctor, forced him to learn the Marleyan version of history, which frames Eldians as descendants of a devil who committed ethnic cleansing. Years later, Grisha joined the Eldian Restorationists after learning Faye was murdered by soldiers for amusement. He married Dina Fritz, a royal descendant, and they had a son, Zeke. When Marley announced a program to train Eldian children as 'Warriors' to infiltrate Paradis Island and seize the Founding Titan, Grisha pressured Zeke to become a double agent for the Restorationists. However, at age seven, Zeke betrayed his parents to the Marleyan authorities, leading to the arrest of the entire resistance cell and their exile to Paradis Island to be turned into Pure Titans.
key_events: Grisha and Faye Yeager leave the Liberio internment zone, resulting in Faye's death at the hands of Marleyan soldiers.
Grisha is indoctrinated with the Marleyan historical narrative regarding Ymir Fritz and the Eldian Empire.
Grisha joins the Eldian Restorationists and marries Dina Fritz, a descendant of the royal family.
The Restorationists learn that Marley fabricated a declaration of war from King Fritz to justify invading Paradis Island for its natural resources.
Grisha attempts to use his son, Zeke, as a double agent within the Marleyan Warrior program.
Zeke betrays his parents to the Marleyan government, resulting in the arrest of the Restorationists and their sentencing to Paradis Island.

summary_text: In a flashback, Grisha Yeager and the Eldian Restorationists are taken to the shores of Paradis Island by Marleyan officers to be executed by transformation into Pure Titans. Grisha recognizes the officer in charge, Eren Kruger, as the man who beat him during his childhood. Sergeant Major Gross, the man who fed Grisha's sister Faye to dogs, forces the Restorationists to transform and hunt down Grice. Grisha realizes that Kruger intentionally suppressed the information regarding Dina Fritz’s royal blood to prevent her from being used by Marley. Dina is transformed into a Titan, and Grisha is left to face a duel against another Titan. Gross reveals his genocidal hatred for Eldians, comparing them to vermin. Just as Gross prepares to throw Grisha to the Titans, Kruger intervenes, pushing Gross to be eaten. Kruger reveals himself as 'The Owl,' injures his hand, and transforms into the Attack Titan, destroying the Marleyan ship. In the present, Eren wakes up in a prison cell, realizing through his father's memories that the Titan who devoured his mother, Carla, was Dina Fritz.
key_events: The Eldian Restorationists are brought to Paradis Island to be turned into Pure Titans.
Grisha identifies Sergeant Major Gross as the man who murdered his sister, Faye.
Dina Fritz is injected with Titan serum and transforms into a Titan.
Eren Kruger reveals his identity as 'The Owl' and transforms into the Attack Titan to destroy the Marleyan ship.
Eren Yeager awakens in a cell and realizes that Dina Fritz's Titan was the one that killed his mother.

summary_text: In a flashback, Eren Kruger reveals himself to Grisha Yeager as an Eldian infiltrator and the inheritor of the Attack Titan. Kruger explains that he is nearing the end of his thirteen-year lifespan—the 'Curse of Ymir'—and tasks Grisha with infiltrating Paradis Island to seize the Founding Titan. He reveals that all Subjects of Ymir are connected by invisible 'paths' that converge at the Founding Titan, which serves as the Coordinate. Kruger justifies his past atrocities, including the sacrifice of Dina Fritz, as necessary steps to ensure the survival of Eldia. He ultimately convinces a hesitant Grisha to accept the mission by emphasizing that their actions and the resulting deaths are inescapable burdens that must be carried forward. Before passing on his Titan power, Kruger reveals that his Titan is known as the 'Attack Titan,' which is defined by its constant pursuit of freedom. In the present, Eren Yeager recounts these memories to Armin Arlert, noting that he and Armin have limited time left to live due to the curse.
key_events: Eren Kruger reveals his identity as an Eldian and the holder of the Attack Titan.
Kruger explains the 'Curse of Ymir' and the 13-year limit on Titan shifters.
The concept of 'paths' and the 'Coordinate' (the Founding Titan) is introduced.
Kruger tasks Grisha Yeager with stealing the Founding Titan on Paradis Island.
Grisha inherits the Attack Titan from Kruger.
Eren shares these memories with Armin, confirming their own limited lifespans.

summary_text: Eren and Mikasa are released from jail and reunite with their comrades, including Queen Historia, who reads a final letter from Ymir detailing her past as a cult figure and her eventual transformation into a Titan. The military leadership convenes to discuss the findings from Grisha Yeager's basement, where Hange reveals that the world views the Eldians as enemies who must be eradicated. During the meeting, Eren experiences memories of his father's encounter with Eren Kruger. He realizes that he was only able to activate the Founding Titan previously because he made physical contact with Dina Fritz, a member of the royal family in her Titan form. Fearing that revealing this would endanger Historia, who is the only remaining royal contact, Eren keeps his discovery a secret, though he suspects that touching a royal Titan is the key to wielding the Founding Titan's power. The chapter concludes with flashbacks of Kruger urging Grisha to start a family within the Walls to save Mikasa and Armin, referencing memories that even Kruger does not fully understand.
key_events: Eren and Mikasa are released from prison and meet with Queen Historia.
Historia reads Ymir's letter, which recounts Ymir's origin as a cult-worshipped beggar and her transformation into a Pure Titan.
The military holds a secret meeting to discuss the contents of Grisha Yeager's basement, identifying the world as the enemy of the Eldians.
Eren realizes that physical contact with a royal-blooded Titan is the trigger for using the Founding Titan.
Eren decides to keep his discovery about the royal bloodline secret to protect Historia.
Flashbacks reveal Eren Kruger instructing Grisha to find love within the Walls to save Mikasa and Armin.

summary_text: Following the discovery of the truth in the basement, the military leadership decides to make the information public despite the risk of social unrest. The Survey Corps faces internal friction as Floch Forster openly criticizes the decision to save Armin Arlert over Erwin Smith, leading to a tense confrontation with the 104th recruits. During an awards ceremony, Eren Yeager touches Historia Reiss's hand, triggering a vivid memory of his father, Grisha, confronting the Reiss family at the chapel. Over the following year, the military clears the Titans from Wall Maria, allowing for the repopulation of Shiganshina. The Survey Corps eventually launches an expedition beyond the walls, successfully reaching the ocean. While his comrades celebrate the discovery, a melancholic Eren realizes that true freedom remains elusive as long as their enemies in Marley exist across the sea.
key_events: The military leadership votes to disclose the truth about the outside world to the public.
Floch Forster confronts the 104th recruits, criticizing the choice to save Armin instead of Erwin.
Eren experiences a memory of Grisha Yeager at the Reiss Chapel upon touching Historia's hand.
Wall Maria is cleared of Titans, and Shiganshina is repopulated.
The Survey Corps reaches the ocean, marking the end of their long-held goal.

summary_text: Set four years after the battle of Shiganshina, the nation of Marley is engaged in a war against the Mid-East Allied Forces. Eldian Warrior candidates, including Gabi Braun and Falco Grice, are deployed on the front lines at Fort Slava to prove their worthiness to inherit the Nine Titans. Marleyan Commander Theo Magath is hesitant to deploy the Jaw and Cart Titans due to the enemy's new anti-Titan artillery, which is mounted on an armored train capable of destroying a Titan in a single shot. Gabi volunteers to neutralize the threat by infiltrating the enemy lines in plainclothes. She successfully destroys the train's tracks with a hidden bomb, prompting the Jaw Titan, Porco Galliard, to enter the fray and shield the candidates from machine-gun fire while decimating the fort's defenses.
key_events: Marleyan forces and Eldian Warrior candidates engage the Mid-East Allied Forces at Fort Slava.
Commander Magath reveals the existence of anti-Titan artillery, preventing the immediate deployment of the Jaw and Cart Titans.
Gabi Braun infiltrates enemy lines in plainclothes and destroys the armored train carrying the anti-Titan artillery.
Porco Galliard transforms into the Jaw Titan to protect the Warrior candidates and assault the fort's defenses.

summary_text: The Marleyan military, supported by the Warrior Unit, launches a final assault on Fort Slava to conclude the four-year war against the Mid-East Allied Forces. Porco Galliard (Jaw Titan) and Pieck Finger (Cart Titan) lead the initial ground charge, while Falco Grice attempts to save an enemy soldier, only to be met with hatred. Commander Theo Magath orders a withdrawal of ground troops to facilitate an aerial strike. Zeke Yeager drops Eldian captives from an airship, transforming them into Pure Titans mid-air to devastate the fort. Reiner Braun (Armored Titan) joins the fray to destroy anti-Titan artillery, though he discovers the enemy's new weaponry can pierce his armor. Zeke transforms into the Beast Titan to destroy the Mid-East naval fleet, narrowly surviving an artillery barrage thanks to Reiner shielding him. The battle ends in a Marleyan victory and a subsequent peace treaty, though the vulnerability of the Titans to modern technology convinces Marley that they must prioritize reclaiming the Founding Titan.
key_events: The Warrior Unit leads a ground assault on Fort Slava.
Zeke Yeager executes an aerial Titan bombardment using Eldian captives.
Reiner Braun and Porco Galliard destroy the fort's anti-Titan artillery.
Zeke Yeager destroys the Mid-East Allied naval fleet.
Reiner Braun shields Zeke from enemy artillery fire.
Marley and the Mid-East Allied Forces sign a peace treaty, highlighting the obsolescence of Titan power against modern weaponry.

summary_text: Following the victory at Fort Slava, Marleyan military leaders discuss the declining strategic value of Titans in an era of advanced naval and aerial warfare. Zeke Yeager proposes a renewed invasion of Paradis Island to secure the Founding Titan, aiming to restore Marley's global dominance while he serves out his final year. Zeke reveals that Paradis has successfully repelled all Marleyan scouting ships using the Attack Titan, anti-Titan weaponry, and the formidable Ackerman clan. Meanwhile, Reiner Braun struggles with trauma and the resentment of Porco Galliard, who blames Reiner for his brother Marcel's death. Reiner spends time with the Warrior candidates, where he observes their similarities to his former comrades. During the return trip to Liberio, Reiner urges a reluctant Falco Grice to surpass Gabi Braun in training and inherit the Armored Titan, hoping to spare Gabi from the short lifespan associated with the power of the Titans.
key_events: Marleyan military leaders acknowledge that Titan power is becoming obsolete due to technological advancements.
Zeke Yeager convinces the military to resume the Paradis Island operation to capture the Founding Titan.
Zeke identifies the threats on Paradis: the Attack Titan, ODM gear, Thunder Spears, and the Ackermans.
Porco Galliard confronts Reiner about his past failures and reveals he inherited Ymir's memories.
Reiner encourages Falco to inherit the Armored Titan to prevent Gabi from suffering the curse of the Nine Titans.

summary_text: The Warrior Unit returns to the Liberio internment zone, where they are reunited with their families. Gabi Braun is celebrated for her role in the victory at Fort Slava, while Falco Grice encounters a traumatized, wounded soldier and attempts to assist him. During a family dinner, Reiner Braun recounts his time on Paradis Island, describing his former comrades as complex individuals rather than simple monsters, much to the confusion of his family, who view the islanders as pure evil. In a series of flashbacks, Reiner recalls his childhood as a Warrior candidate, revealing his motivation for joining the program: to become an honorary Marleyan and reunite his mother with his estranged Marleyan father. The chapter concludes with a glimpse of a young Eren Yeager on Paradis Island, while the flashback shows a young Reiner expressing his desire to become a hero by defeating the 'devils' of the island.
key_events: The Warrior Unit returns to Liberio and reunites with their families.
Falco Grice encounters a wounded soldier and notices his armband is worn incorrectly.
Reiner Braun describes his experiences on Paradis Island to his family, characterizing his former comrades as individuals rather than just enemies.
Reiner's mother reinforces the Marleyan propaganda that the inhabitants of Paradis are evil and must be destroyed.
Flashbacks reveal Reiner's childhood motivation for becoming a Warrior: to reunite his parents and gain status as an honorary Marleyan.
Young Reiner expresses his resolve to become a hero by destroying the people of Paradis Island.

summary_text: In the Liberio internment zone, Reiner Braun struggles with his mental state following his return from Paradis Island, a change noticed by his cousin Gabi. Zeke Yeager convenes a meeting with the Warrior unit to discuss the declining strategic value of Titans due to advancements in conventional weaponry. To secure the future of Eldians and maintain Marley's dominance, Zeke proposes a plan to retake the Founding Titan from Paradis Island, utilizing the Tybur family—holders of the War Hammer Titan—as diplomatic spokespeople to improve the world's perception of Eldians. In a series of flashbacks, Reiner recalls his early days as a Warrior candidate. He remembers being chosen for the Armored Titan, his father's rejection of him due to his Eldian heritage, and the initial mission to Paradis. During the journey to the island, Marcel Galliard confessed to Reiner that he had manipulated the selection process to ensure Reiner was chosen over his own brother, Porco, to protect the latter. Shortly after this revelation, a Titan attacked the group, and Marcel sacrificed himself to save Reiner, leaving the remaining Warriors to flee in terror.
key_events: Zeke Yeager outlines a plan to use the Tybur family to justify a renewed assault on Paradis Island.
The Warrior unit is informed that conventional military technology is rendering Titan power obsolete.
Reiner recalls his father rejecting him after learning of his Eldian heritage.
Marcel Galliard reveals to Reiner that he sabotaged the selection process to keep his brother, Porco, from becoming the Armored Titan.
Marcel is devoured by a Titan while saving Reiner during their initial infiltration of Paradis Island.

summary_text: Following the death of Marcel Galliard, Reiner, Bertolt, and Annie struggle with the failure of their mission. While Annie advocates for returning to Marley, Reiner insists they must succeed to avoid punishment, eventually convincing them to continue the assault on Paradis. The trio reaches Wall Maria, where Bertolt transforms into the Colossus Titan to breach the gate. Reiner then uses his Armored Titan form to breach the inner gate of Shiganshina. After infiltrating the refugee population, the three discuss the nature of the Fritz monarchy and the risk of the Founding Titan unleashing the Wall Titans. To gain access to the royal family, they decide to enlist in the military, eventually joining the 104th Training Corps, where Reiner declares his intent to 'save humanity.'
key_events: Reiner convinces Annie and Bertolt to continue the mission despite Marcel's death.
Bertolt breaches the gate of Wall Maria using the Colossus Titan.
Reiner breaches the inner gate of Shiganshina using the Armored Titan.
The trio infiltrates the refugee population and learns about the Fritz family's status.
The Warriors decide to join the 104th Training Corps to infiltrate the Military Police.

summary_text: The chapter alternates between flashbacks of the Warriors' initial mission on Paradis and current events in Marley. In the past, Annie's failed attempt to track the Reiss family leads the trio to decide to breach Wall Rose during the 104th Training Corps' graduation. Reiner, struggling with his dual identity and the guilt of his mission, contemplates suicide but is stopped by the sight of the new generation of Warrior candidates. In the present, Falco Grice interacts with a wounded, traumatized soldier named 'Mister Kruger' (Eren Yeager), who encourages him to live a long life rather than inherit a Titan. Meanwhile, Willy Tybur meets with Theo Magath, revealing that the Tybur family has been the true power behind Marley all along. Willy admits his family's negligence has led to the current crisis and plans to expose the truth at an upcoming festival. The chapter concludes with Falco unknowingly mailing a letter for Eren, who is using the boy to communicate with his allies outside the internment zone.
key_events: Annie Leonhart is caught by Kenny Ackerman while tailing a member of the Military Police.
Reiner, Bertolt, and Annie resolve to breach Wall Rose to force the King of the Walls out of hiding.
Reiner nearly commits suicide in the present but is deterred by his responsibility toward the new Warrior candidates.
Eren Yeager, disguised as a wounded soldier, manipulates Falco Grice into mailing a letter for him.
Willy Tybur reveals to Theo Magath that the Tybur family are the true rulers of Marley and plans to make a major announcement at an upcoming festival.

summary_text: One month after the Marley Mid-East War, the Warrior candidates continue their training in Liberio while the Marleyan military prepares for an upcoming festival. Willy Tybur coordinates with Commander Magath to consolidate military power and address the 'Titan problem,' while secretly identifying that there are infiltrators within their ranks. Meanwhile, Eren Yeager, disguised as the wounded soldier 'Kruger,' interacts with Grisha Yeager's father, who expresses deep regret over his past treatment of his children. As the festival begins, the candidates enjoy the festivities, unaware of the impending political shift. The chapter concludes when Falco leads a reluctant Reiner Braun to a basement meeting, where Reiner comes face-to-face with Eren Yeager, who has successfully infiltrated the internment zone.
key_events: Reiner and the other Warriors discuss the future of the Paradis invasion strategy.
Willy Tybur and Theo Magath discuss the upcoming purge of Marley's military leadership and the presence of 'rats' in their midst.
Eren Yeager (as Kruger) speaks with Grisha's father, who reveals his lingering guilt over the deaths of his children.
Willy Tybur announces to international ambassadors that he will reveal a final solution to the Titan crisis.
Falco leads Reiner to a basement where Reiner discovers that 'Kruger' is actually Eren Yeager.

summary_text: In Marley, Eren Yeager forces a terrified Reiner Braun to sit with him and Falco Grice in a basement beneath the festival stage, subtly threatening the civilians above to ensure Reiner's compliance. Meanwhile, Willy Tybur prepares to deliver a historic speech to the world's leaders. As the ceremony begins, Pieck Finger and Porco Galliard are lured into a trap by a soldier and dropped into an underground cell. On stage, Willy reveals the true history of the Eldian Empire: King Fritz was a pacifist who orchestrated the empire's collapse and fabricated the hero Helos to protect Marley, intending for his people to eventually be destroyed. Willy concludes by announcing that this peace has been shattered by a rebellion on Paradis Island, identifying Eren Yeager as the new holder of the Founding Titan and a threat to global peace.
